Burrow.description: Burrow is a Speed Skill that defines how quickly the Pokémon can move underground. The number paired with Burrow represents how many spaces/meters the Pokémon can Shift during a round of combat underground. The holes dug are only as large as the Pokémon who burrows. While Burrowing, the Pokémon has knowledge of its underground surroundings up to X meters away, where X is its Burrow value. If a Pokémon learns the Move Dig and does not have the Burrow Skill, they gain Burrow 3. If they already have the Burrow Skill, the Burrow value is raised 3.

Intelligence.description: The Intelligence Skill defines the Pokémon's competence when it comes to simple thought. The smarter the Pokémon is, the easier it is to act on their own accord if you can't command them. Intelligence 1: Feeble-mindedness (slow reaction time, unable to do simple tasks). Intelligence 2: Deficiency (self-aware). Intelligence 3: Dullness (can't figure out tasks alone but can follow others). Intelligence 4: Normal (can build and use tools). Intelligence 5: Superior (average human intellect). Intelligence 6: Vastly Superior (able to function as a leader and act by itself). Intelligence 7: Genius (super computer thought, speaks human languages).

Jump.description: The Jump Skill states the max height the Pokémon can jump into the air in a single bound. If your Pokémon utilizes their Jump Skill during a round, it counts as their Shift for that round. Jump 1: 3 ft/1 m. Jump 2: 6 ft/1.8 m. Jump 3: 10 ft/3 m. Jump 4: 15 ft/4.5 m. Jump 5: 20 ft/6 m. Jump 6: 25 ft/7.6 m. Jump 7: 35 ft/10.6 m. Jump 8: 50 ft/15.2 m. Jump 9: 70 ft/21 m. Jump 10: 100 ft/30.5 m. If a Pokémon learns the Move Bounce, their Jump value is raised 1.

Overland.description: Overland is a Speed Skill that defines how quickly the Pokémon moves while on dry land. The number paired with Overland represents how many spaces/meters the Pokémon can Shift over normal terrain during a round of combat.

Power.description: Power represents a Pokémon's strength. The number corresponds to a weight that the Pokémon should be able to lift. Based on how much they can lift, you can estimate how much they can push or force into their strikes. Power 1: 10 lbs/5 kg. Power 2: 50 lbs/23 kg. Power 3: 100 lbs/45 kg. Power 4: 200 lbs/90 kg. Power 5: 350 lbs/158 kg. Power 6: 500 lbs/227 kg. Power 7: 750 lbs/340 kg. Power 8: 1000 lbs/455 kg. Power 9: 2500 lbs/1135 kg. Power 10: 4000 lbs/1815 kg. If a Pokémon learns the Move Strength, their Power value is raised 1.

Sky.description: Sky is a Speed Skill that defines how quickly the Pokémon moves while flying, floating, or levitating. The number represents how many spaces/meters the Pokémon can Shift during a round of combat in the air. If a Pokémon has the Sky skill, it can propel itself off the ground for at least 5 minutes at a time. If a Pokémon learns the Move Fly and does not have the Sky Skill, they gain Sky 4. If they already have the Sky Skill, the Sky value is raised 4.

Surface.description: Surface is a Speed Skill that defines how quickly the Pokémon moves while on the surface of water to the Pokémon's height below the surface. The number represents how many spaces/meters the Pokémon can Shift during a round of combat. If a Pokémon learns the Move Surf and does not have the Surface Skill, they gain Surface 4. If they already have the Surface Skill, the Surface value is raised 4. If the Pokémon had the Sinker Skill before learning Surf, they lose their Sinker Skill.

Underwater.description: Underwater is a Speed Skill that defines how quickly the Pokémon can move underwater. The number represents how many spaces/meters the Pokémon can Shift during a round of combat underwater. If the Pokémon does not have the Gilled Skill, they must surface at least once every 4 rounds no matter how high their Underwater Skill's value is. If a Pokémon learns the Move Dive and does not have the Underwater Skill, they gain Underwater 3. If they already have the Underwater Skill, the Underwater value is raised 3.

Alluring.description: Pokémon who are Alluring smell very pleasant. They may attract wild Pokémon easily. Attention is commonly turned toward fragrant, Alluring Pokémon by wilds. If a Pokémon learns the Move Sweet Scent and does not have the Alluring Skill, they gain Alluring.

Amorphous.description: Amorphous Pokémon have an inconsistent shape. They can flatten and reform themselves like water. They can stretch out their body material and condense themselves as well. By doing this, a Pokémon can access places others couldn't, or bypass a door or two, only to let their non-Amorphous friends in afterwards.

Aura.description: Pokémon who can use Aura are blessed with a great power. They can project their thoughts to Pokémon and Humans, and can read a person's Aura (but not their thoughts). Everything has an Aura, but not many can access it. Auras can be any color—if tinted, the target has little negative intentions; if blackened, the target has malicious intentions. If a Pokémon learns Aura Sphere, Dragon Pulse, Dark Pulse, Focus Blast, Force Palm, Heal Pulse, Detect, or Vacuum Wave and does not have the Aura Skill, they gain Aura.

Blender.description: A Pokémon with Blender is an advanced Stealth user. While Shifting, unless they purposely want to, Blender Pokémon do not make any noise. Unless they have used a Ranged Move during the current round or previous round, Ranged Moves may not target them if the line of sight goes through Rough Terrain or if the Pokémon is on Rough Terrain. Whenever a Blender is targeted by a Move, one must roll +2 during Accuracy Check to hit.

Bloom.description: When a Pokémon with Bloom (Cherrim) is in Sunny weather, it transforms—changing its appearance from the closed bud, purple-petal form into the pink open flower form.

Chilled.description: A Pokémon with the Chilled Skill is always cold when touched.

Climber.description: A Pokémon with the Climber skill treats vertical terrain as normal non-hindering terrain while using their Overland Skill, unless it is specifically a smooth vertical surface.

Dream Smoke.description: A Pokémon who has the Dream Smoke Skill can produce Dream Smoke. Dream Smoke is harvested for Dream Doctors who can manipulate a Pokémon's mind with the use of Dream Smoke and machinery. If a Pokémon learns the Move Dream Eater and does not have the Dream Smoke Skill, they gain Dream Smoke.

Egg Warmer.description: A Pokémon with Egg Warmer can keep an egg safely warmer than the average human. Eggs tend to hatch 2 hours per day faster than average per species when watched over by a Pokémon with this Skill.

Family Ties.description: A Pokémon with Family Ties can naturally make eggs that are from another species: Nidoran F/Nidorina/Nidoqueen can hatch Nidoran F or M; Jynx can hatch Smoochum or Mime Jr.; Miltank can hatch Miltank or Tauros; Plusle/Minun can hatch Plusle or Minun; Illumise can hatch Illumise or Volbeat; Lunatone/Solrock can hatch Lunatone or Solrock.

Firestarter.description: A Pokémon who has the Firestarter Skill can produce flames. They can control how lightly or powerfully they produce fire—from puffs the size of a lighter to a large burst capable of engulfing a tree. If a Pokémon learns Ember, Fire Blast, Fire Spin, Flame Wheel, or Flamethrower and does not have the Firestarter Skill, they gain Firestarter.

Fountain.description: A Pokémon who has the Fountain Skill can produce freshwater. They can control how lightly or powerfully they produce water—from sprinkling to forcefully shooting with the strength of a fire hose. If a Pokémon learns Water Gun, Water Pulse, Hydro Pump, or Water Sport and does not have the Fountain Skill, they gain Fountain.

Fragile.description: Archen and Archeops' default Ability is always Defeatist.

Freezer.description: When a Pokémon has the Freezer Skill, they can target an area on the ground and turn it into Icy Terrain in place of their Move during their turn (EOT). A 1d20 roll determines the amount of ground turned into Icy Terrain. If a Pokémon learns Aurora Beam, Blizzard, Ice Beam, or Sheer Cold and does not have the Freezer Skill, they gain Freezer.

Gilled.description: A Gilled Pokémon can breathe underwater. It never needs to come up for air, remaining underwater for as long as it wants to.

Glow.description: A Pokémon with the Glow Skill can emit light from a part of its body. Depending on the variety of wild Pokémon nearby, it might attract Pokémon or ward them away. If a Pokémon learns Flash or Tail Glow and does not have the Glow Skill, they gain Glow.

Groundshaper.description: A Pokémon with the Groundshaper Skill can transform the terrain around them to create Rough Terrain or flatten out Rough Terrain to create Basic Terrain. In place of the Pokémon's Move during their turn, they can change a 1 meter radius around them (all adjacent spaces) from Basic Earth Terrain into Rough Terrain or vice versa (EOT). If a Pokémon learns Earthquake or Magnitude and does not have the Groundshaper Skill, they gain Groundshaper.

Guster.description: The Guster can create bursts of wind. The power can vary from a light breeze to a powerful burst of air capable of lifting light objects into the air or providing lift for a chute. If a Pokémon learns Gust or Tailwind and does not have the Guster Skill, they gain Guster.

Heater.description: A Pokémon with the Heater Skill is always warm when touched.

Husk.description: Minior's default Ability is always Shields Down.

Icestep.description: Pokémon with the Icestep Skill treat Icy or Snowy terrain as Basic Terrain.

Inflatable.description: A Pokémon with the Skill Inflatable can expand its size up to 125% of its normal size. While Inflated, weight doesn't change. To hit an Inflated Pokémon, you must roll -3 during Accuracy Check. While Inflated, a Pokémon becomes Blocking Terrain—you may not target through it. If a Pokémon learns Growth or Stockpile and does not have the Inflatable Skill, they gain Inflatable.

Invisibility.description: Pokémon who have the Invisibility Skill can turn invisible. Pokémon may not perform Moves while invisible. While invisible, you must roll +4 during Accuracy Checks to hit the Pokémon. When a Pokémon turns invisible, they can only remain invisible for up to 4 minutes. After becoming visible, they must wait two plus the number of minutes they spent invisible before turning invisible again.

Juicer.description: When Shuckle is holding a berry, after 24 hours of that Berry going unused, it will be juiced and stored in the Shuckle's shell. The Berry Juice has all the properties of the berry it was made from, except HP/PP recovery is doubled. If the Berry Juice item is left in the Shuckle's shell unused for two weeks, it will be condensed into a Rare Candy.

Magnetic.description: Magnetic Pokémon can lightly manipulate magnetic fields. With this, they can repel or attract iron and/or steel, holding it to their body or pushing it away. Through this manipulation, they can also feel magnetic fields and discern north. If a Pokémon learns Magnet Rise or Magnet Bomb and does not have the Magnetic Skill, they gain Magnetic.

Materializer.description: The Materializer can form rock (normal sedimentary rock) from nothing—anywhere from pebble to person-sized boulder. For every 5 pounds of rock formed, the Pokémon loses 2 HP. Rock is formed adjacent to the Pokémon and they can only add up to 50 pounds of rock over 1 round in place of a Move (EOT). If a Pokémon learns Rock Wrecker and does not have the Materializer Skill, they gain Materializer.

Mind Lock.description: Pokémon who have the Skill Mind Lock cannot have their minds read.

Pack Mon.description: A Pokémon with Pack Mon instinctively belongs to a rigid pack structure. Wild Pokémon that are of this Pokémon's unevolved form will instantly be considered "Helpful" to you and your group and will do tasks for the party within their power. Helpful Pokémon cannot be ordered to attack without reason, and if capture is attempted it will fail and the Pokémon will run away.

Phasing.description: A Pokémon with Phasing may Shift through Hindering or Blocking Terrain without their Speed Skills being affected. They can turn intangible and move through solid walls or other Pokémon. A Pokémon can remain intangible with Phasing for up to 30 seconds. If a Pokémon remains intangible, they may not perform Moves during their turn.

Reach.description: A Pokémon with Reach may make Melee attacks from up to 5 meters (5 spaces) away from the Pokémon. Pokémon with Reach are either really large or have a stretchy limb with which they can strike foes from a distance.

Schooling.description: Wishiwashi's default Ability is always Schooling.

Shrinkable.description: A Pokémon with the Skill Shrinkable can shrink its size up to 25% of its normal size. While Shrunken, a Pokémon may not perform any Moves. Weight doesn't change. To hit a Shrunken Pokémon, you must roll +3 during Accuracy Check. Using Shrinkable is not the same as using the Move Minimize. If a Pokémon learns Minimize and does not have the Shrinkable Skill, they gain Shrinkable.

Sinker.description: Sinker means the Pokémon cannot swim or move in water. For every round of an encounter the Pokémon is in water deep enough to be submerged, they lose 25% of their max HP. If lowered to -100% HP for more than 10 rounds of combat, make a Death Savings roll as usual. If a Pokémon gains a Surface or Underwater Skill, they lose their Sinker Skill.

Soulless.description: Shedinja is a unique Pokémon. Its default Ability will always be Wonder Guard and Shedinja's Max HP is always 1. You may not add to a Shedinja's HP stat, and Shedinja may not have temporary HP. However, Shedinja never needs to make a Death Saving Throw. Shedinja may not ever have the Ability Solid Rock. Shedinja may not be targeted by Press On!

Sprouter.description: A Pokémon with the Sprouter Skill can rapidly influence the growth of plants and flora around them. Over 5 seconds, a Sprouter can grow a plant 1 meter taller or wider, and can make plants grow up to 125% of normal size. To use Sprouter for 10 seconds, they must sacrifice 1 HP in place of a Move. If a Pokémon learns Ingrain, Natural Gift, Nature Power, Secret Power, or Synthesis and does not have the Sprouter Skill, they gain Sprouter.

Stance.description: Aegislash's default Ability is always Stance Change.

Stealth.description: A Pokémon with the Stealth Skill are excellent at approaching the enemy for an up close assault. While Shifting, unless they purposely want to, Stealth Pokémon do not make any noise. Unless a Pokémon with Stealth has used a Ranged Move during the current round or the round previous, Ranged Moves may not target them if the line of sight drawn goes through Rough Terrain or if the Pokémon is on Rough Terrain.

Sticky Climber.description: A Pokémon with the Sticky Climber skill treats vertical terrain as normal non-hindering terrain while using their Overland Skill, and can even move on ceiling surfaces as normal non-hindering terrain.

Telekinetic.description: Telekinetic Pokémon can move objects with their mind. They can lift things lighter than their level multiplied by 5 pounds. They can target objects up to X meters away, where X is the Pokémon's level. If a Pokémon learns Psychic or Telekinesis and does not have the Telekinetic Skill, they gain Telekinetic.

Telepath.description: A Telepathic Pokémon can read the minds of people and other Pokémon. Telepathic Pokémon can project their thoughts to humans and Pokémon. The Pokémon can only read surface thoughts. The Pokémon cannot read the minds of another Pokémon whose Intelligence is two ranks below or two ranks higher than their own.

Threaded.description: If a Pokémon has the Threaded Skill, they may replace their turn's Shift with a Threaded Shift—targeting an object and shooting a strong silk line or vine (up to 10 meters), then retracting it to pull themselves toward the object (if lighter) or the object toward them. If targeting a creature, Accuracy Check is 6. You may still use a Move on the same turn. If a Pokémon learns Electro Web, Spider Web, String Shot, Vine Whip, or Power Whip and does not have the Threaded Skill, they gain Threaded.

Tracker.description: Pokémon with the Skill Tracker have a strong sense of smell that they can use to follow other Pokémon or people. If the Pokémon has smelt whom they want to track in the past day, they can pursue with a check of 11+ on d20. To pick up a random scent from nothing, 16+. To pick up a specific scent from nothing, 20. May only make these checks once per hour. If a Pokémon learns Odor Sleuth and does not have the Tracker Skill, they gain Tracker.

Troublesome.description: Slakoth and Slaking's default Ability is always Truant.

Weathershape.description: Castform's appearance changes with the weather around it. It changes to its orange form in Sunny weather, its blue and grey form in Raining weather, and its light blue form when it's Hailing.

Weeble.description: Wobbuffett are very limited when it comes to their Move variety. As a result, Wobbuffett can use Counter and Mirror Coat as EOT Frequency Moves rather than Battle Frequency Moves. However, after the first use of Mirror Coat or Counter during an encounter, Wobbuffett's Defense and Special Defense are treated as if they are ten less (-10). This penalty is only active while calculating damage for those moves. If a PP Up is used on Counter or Mirror Coat, Weeble is not needed for that Move anymore.

Wimp.description: Wimpod's default Ability is always Wimp Out.

Wired.description: Wired Pokémon have a special relation to electronic devices. They can enter machines and travel through connected electronics through any cords that connect them instantly. While inside machines, they can read data on the machine on a check of Daily. While inside machines, they can take control of the machine on a check of 16 on d20 once per hour.

Wormadam's Coat.description: When a female Burmy evolves, it gains either the type Grass, Ground, or Steel. Its new type (and the Moves it will have access to) are based on surroundings at the time of evolution. If surrounded by natural greenery: Grass type; by sandy or rocky terrain: Ground type; if by industrial or urban surroundings: Steel type.

Zapper.description: Pokémon with the Zapper Skill can produce controlled electricity or send electrical currents through various conductive materials. If an electrical device is capable of being recharged, a Pokémon with Zapper can recharge it to full power on a check of 13+ on 1d20 Daily. If a Pokémon learns Charge, Discharge, Thunderbolt, or Thundershock and does not have the Zapper Skill, they gain Zapper.

All for One.description: The Swords of Justice work together very naturally. When within 10 spaces (10m) of another Pokémon with All for One, this Pokémon rolls an additional d20 per other Pokémon with All for One.

Avatar.description: Some legendary Pokémon can change their form into a human-like body that retains their power but would fool anyone who does not know of the Pokémon's disguise. In this human form, they may retain all Skills, Abilities, and Moves.

Extinguisher.description: The Extinguisher can calm an erupting volcano by making a check of 20+ on 2d20 every hour. Once calmed, the Extinguisher can settle it so as not to let it erupt for at least 1d4 years, or the Extinguisher can release a dormant volcano with the same roll, letting it erupt again.

Force of Life.description: Pokémon with Force of Life can give and take life from other beings, rapidly, at a massive scale. They are not compelled to do so, but when they choose to, all forms of life within line of sight to the Pokémon lose or gain 15% of their max HP per round.

Impenetrable.description: A Pokémon with Impenetrable has an unbreakable barrier around their skin. It subtracts an additional 40 damage from all Moves that hit them. Impenetrable can't be altered further to have even more resistance to harm, or less.

Mindslaver.description: Mewtwo can have complete control of up to 10 Pokémon or Trainers at any given time (called Mindslaves). Once per day, a roll of 39+ (adding any one Modifier) on 2d20 will free a Mindslave. To give a target the Mindslave title, Mewtwo must make a Melee attack in place of a Move and roll 9+ on 1d20. Mindslaves can be controlled from any distance. Mewtwo can experience everything a Mindslave does.

Multiform.description: Multiform Pokémon may change between their forms at will. Prepare stats for any forms if you intend to use a Multiform Pokémon.

Power of the Land.description: The Tapu are instinctive beings that are in tune with the space around them. As long as they are fighting within the land they protect, attackers must first roll a 16 on 1d20 before targeting the Tapu with a Move, Skill, or Feature.

Realm Ruler.description: Realm Rulers own their own space in reality where they rule as a divine being. It is a separate realm of existence that is incredibly difficult to reach. Arceus is the ruler of the Realm of Creation. Dialga is the ruler of the Realm of Time. Giratina is the ruler of the Reverse World. Palkia is the ruler of the Realm of Space. It is possible for very powerful Legendaries to have other realms as well.

Space Bender.description: Pokémon with Space Bender can move objects that they have knowledge of from any point to any point within line of sight. They can do this to unwilling targets on a check of 16 on 1d20.

Tide Caller.description: The Tide Caller can calm an active tsunami or tidal wave by making a check of 20+ on 2d20 every hour. Once calmed, the Tide Caller can settle the ocean, or the Tide Caller can raise another tidal wave or tsunami with the same roll again.

Time Traveler.description: A Time Traveler can go back and forth through time. Usually, Time Travelers will need an anchor point to return to when leaving a particular moment in Time that could be considered their "real place in time." A Time Traveler can also stop time for anyone other than themselves (EOT) for up to 1d4 minutes.

Ultra Beast.description: Ultra Beasts are Pokémon from another dimension—a completely different universe where rules and physics do not exist in the same way as in the Pokémon world, yet Poké Balls can still potentially affect them. If an Ultra Beast is encountered, a dangerous hole into that world must have opened. Ultra Beasts always begin an encounter with each stat raised 2 Combat Stages.

Wish Granter.description: Jirachi has three tags on its body, which will grant any wish that is written on them. Only one wish will be granted per tag. A wish on a tag will remain on the tag for 1d100 + 50 years, at which point the written wish will fade. If the wish alters reality in any way, it is possible that the wish will have very bad repercussions and on rare occasions it is possible the wish will not be granted.

Ace Trainer.description: Masters of battle strategy who inspire their Pokémon to victory through superior tactics and training.

Breeder.description: Patient caretakers who raise Pokémon with love, specializing in eggs, evolution, and nurturing growth.

Capture Specialist.description: Tech experts who modify Poké Balls and master advanced capture techniques.

Coordinator.description: Performers who showcase Pokémon beauty and skill in contests and performances.

Martial Artist.description: Fighters who train their bodies to battle alongside their Pokémon in combat.

Mystic.description: Those who channel ancient and supernatural powers to bond with Pokémon and the spirit world.

Psychic.description: Individuals with mental powers who use telekinesis, telepathy, and other psionic abilities.

Ranger.description: Nature guardians who protect wild Pokémon and excel in wilderness survival.

Researcher.description: Scholars who study Pokémon behavior, biology, and the mysteries of the world.

Chaser.description: Specializes in pursuing and hunting down specific targets.

Enduring Soul.description: Masters of stamina who outlast opponents in prolonged battles.

Stat Ace.description: Experts at maximizing specific stats for optimal performance.

Strategist.description: Tactical geniuses who plan several moves ahead.

Tag Battler.description: Specialists in double battles and team coordination.

Type Ace.description: Masters of a specific Pokémon type.

Underdog.description: Fighters who excel when facing stronger opponents.

Botanist.description: Specialists in Grass-type Pokémon and plant cultivation.

Chef.description: Masters of Pokémon food and beneficial cooking.

Evolver.description: Experts in triggering and controlling Pokémon evolution.

Groomer.description: Specialists in Pokémon care and appearance.

Hatcher.description: Masters of egg breeding and hatching techniques.

Medic.description: Healers who treat Pokémon injuries and ailments.

Move Tutor.description: Teachers who help Pokémon learn new moves.

Artificer.description: Crafters of specialized tools and devices.

Collector.description: Dedicated to completing collections of rare Pokémon.

Engineer.description: Technical experts who build and modify equipment.

Juggler.description: Masters of handling multiple Poké Balls simultaneously.

Pok\u00e9ball Designer.description: Creators of custom Poké Ball designs.

Trapper.description: Experts in setting traps for wild Pokémon.

Choreographer.description: Masters of dance and movement routines.

Fashion Designer.description: Creators of stylish Pokémon accessories and outfits.

Athlete.description: Peak physical performers in various sports.

Aura User.description: Warriors who harness the power of Aura.

Dirty Fighter.description: Combat pragmatists who fight without rules.

Yogi.description: Masters of meditation and inner peace.

Bard.description: Musicians who channel power through song and music.

Bodysnatcher.description: Those who can possess or swap bodies.

Conduit.description: Channels for spiritual and elemental energy.

Godspeaker.description: Those who communicate with legendary Pokémon.

Shaman.description: Spirit mediums who commune with the other side.

Air Adept.description: Psychics who control wind and air currents.

Earth Shaker.description: Psychics who manipulate the ground and earth.

Empath.description: Those who sense and influence emotions.

Fire Breather.description: Psychics who create and control flames.

Hex Maniac.description: Dark psychics who specialize in curses.

Rain Waker.description: Psychics who control water and weather.

Coach.description: Trainers who specialize in teaching others.

Commander.description: Leaders who direct Pokémon in tactical formations.

Detective.description: Investigators who solve mysteries with Pokémon.

Rider.description: Experts in mounted Pokémon travel and combat.

Signer.description: Those who form special bonds through marks.

Cryptozoologist.description: Seekers of rare and legendary Pokémon.

Dream Doctor.description: Specialists in dream manipulation and study.

Petrologist.description: Experts in fossils and ancient Pokémon.

Photographer.description: Artists who capture Pokémon through photography.

Watcher.description: Observers who study Pokémon behavior in nature.

Aim for the Horn!.frequency: Daily – Every 4 levels gained, +1 use per day
Aim for the Horn!.target: Your Pokémon targeting with a Move
Aim for the Horn!.effect: On a roll of 19 or 20 during Accuracy Check, your Pokémon's attack will deal Neutral damage to a target regardless of Resistances or Immunities.

Aim for the Horn! +.frequency: Daily – Every 4 levels gained, +1 use per day
Aim for the Horn! +.target: Your Pokémon targeting with a Move
Aim for the Horn! +.effect: On a roll of 16-20 during Accuracy Check, your Pokémon's attack will deal Neutral damage to a target regardless of Resistances or Immunities. Replaces Aim for the Horn!

Back Off.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, +1 use per day
Back Off.trigger: A Trainer, not involved in a Gym or Competition battle, challenges you
Back Off.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the result is higher than 10, the Trainer is intimidated and withdraws their challenge.

Chosen One.trigger: You fail to roll high enough for any type of check
Chosen One.effect: You don't fail the check. You may not apply this to a Pokémon's check.

Dual Wielding.prerequisites: Level 10, 12 HP, 12 ATK, 12 DEF, 12 SATK, 12 SDEF, 12 SPD
Dual Wielding.effect: You may control 2 Pokémon at the same time. During a round of battle, they are each included in the battle queue. You are still only allotted 1 Trainer Action. Cannot use with Multitasking in the same round.

Give It Your All.frequency: One Time Use Only – +1 use per 10 levels gained
Give It Your All.effect: Target one of your Pokémon's Moves. For one use, it is a Critical Hit that cannot miss.

Hey Guys, Watch This.effect: Instead of gaining a new Feature when you level up, you gain 1 Feat Point. A Feat Point can be spent at any time you can use a Trainer Action (doesn't take an action). When spent, add any Feature whose prerequisites you meet. Cannot regain spent Feat Points.

I Can Take a Hit.effect: When taking damage from anything reduce that damage by 5. Does not reduce the cost of activating Features that require HP loss.

I Can Take a Hit +.effect: When taking damage from anything reduce that damage by 10. Does not reduce the cost of activating Features that require HP loss. Replaces I Can Take a Hit.

I Believe in You!.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, +1 use per day
I Believe in You!.effect: For the remainder of combat, your Pokémon deals an additional 1d6 during the Move's damage for each point you have in SDEF modifier.

League Member.effect: Become a Frontier Brain or Gym Leader. Must accept challenges weekly. If you lose, give victor a Medal/Badge. Paid 2000₽/week (Leaders) or 4500₽/week (Brains) if accepting 3+ challenges. Add 2 to your SDEF stat.

Let Me Help You With That.frequency: Daily – Every 4 levels gained, +1 use per day
Let Me Help You With That.target: An allied Trainer making a check
Let Me Help You With That.effect: The ally has +2 added to their check.

Let Me Help You With That +.prerequisites: Let Me Help You With That, Level 10
Let Me Help You With That +.frequency: Daily – Every 7 levels gained, +1 use per day
Let Me Help You With That +.target: An allied Trainer making a check
Let Me Help You With That +.effect: The ally has +5 added to their check. Replaces Let Me Help You With That.

Let's Get That Lock Open.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, +1 use per day
Let's Get That Lock Open.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SATK modifier. If the result is higher than 15, the lock is unlocked and is undamaged and doesn't appear tampered with.

Look Out!.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, +1 use per day
Look Out!.effect: During this round, if your targeted Pokémon is attacked, flip a coin until you flip tails. The foe must roll 1 higher during Accuracy Check to hit your Pokémon for each heads.

Mega Evolver.prerequisites: 10 HP, 10 ATK, 10 DEF, 10 SATK, 10 SDEF, 10 SPD, a Key Stone
Mega Evolver.target: A Pokémon with a Mega Stone
Mega Evolver.effect: Mega Evolve the Pokémon for 10 minutes or until it is returned while under 0 HP.

Multitasking.prerequisites: Level 10, 12 HP, 12 ATK, 12 DEF, 12 SATK, 12 SDEF, 12 SPD
Multitasking.effect: During encounters, you may perform 2 different Trainer Actions per round. You may not use more than one Trainer Action related Feature per round. Cannot use with Dual Wielding in the same round.

Not Yet!.trigger: Your Pokémon is lowered to 0 HP or less, but not greater than -100% HP
Not Yet!.effect: Before fainting, the targeted Pokémon can make one last shift and Move and then immediately faints afterwards. Cannot be used with Endeavor, Explosion, Flail, Pain Split, Reversal or Self-destruct.

Random Knowledge.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, +1 use per day
Random Knowledge.target: Anything you have line of sight to, or an idea/phrase you just heard
Random Knowledge.effect: Roll d20 and add your SATK and SDEF modifiers. If you roll higher than 14, you know about the thing you targeted. If targeting a Pokémon, you must have already scanned it with a Pokédex.

Remedial First Aid.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, +1 use per day
Remedial First Aid.effect: Roll 1d20 and Add your SATK or SDEF Modifier. Heal the target this much HP.

Satoshi's Karma.prerequisites: Satoshi's Luck, released 3 fully-evolved loyal Pokémon
Satoshi's Karma.effect: You may re-roll any single die. Replaces Satoshi's Luck.

Satoshi's Luck.effect: At the cost of 15 HP (cannot be reduced), you may re-roll any single die.

Step Aside!.frequency: One Time Use Only – +1 use per 10 levels gained
Step Aside!.trigger: Your Pokémon is targeted by a Move or Trainer Attack
Step Aside!.effect: Your Pokémon immediately Shifts. If it Shifts away from a targeted area, the Move or Trainer Attack is avoided. If unable to shift out of the way, it takes no damage. Only "Spirit Surge" effects may still activate.

Study Session.prerequisites: Purchased a book at least once
Study Session.effect: Must buy a book for 70₽ at each new town's PokéMart. Your Special Attack OR Special Defense stat gains 1 point. Can take multiple times (still only 70₽ per book).

Voltorb Flip.prerequisites: 12 HP Stat or 12 SATK or 12 SDEF
Voltorb Flip.trigger: A human or Pokémon tries to read your mind
Voltorb Flip.effect: Roll 1d20 + SATK or SDEF modifier. If 13+, you think of Voltorb Flip and block the mind reader. If successful, mind reading attempts fail for the next hour.

Workout.effect: Must exercise at least once every 3 days (30 min, lose 10 HP). Your HP, ATK, DEF, or SPD stat gains 1 point. Can take multiple times (still only lose 10 HP total).

Use Pok\u00e9dex.target: A Pokémon not yet identified within 10m
Use Pok\u00e9dex.effect: Point your Pokédex at the unidentified Pokémon to identify it (takes 12 seconds or 2 combat rounds). For every 40 Pokémon identified, your Trainer Levels Up!

Arms User.effect: Your Accuracy Check for Arms attacks is 6. Add highest of ATK or SPD modifiers to damage. "Damage": DB1 (1d10+4), DB2 at Lv7+ (1d12+6), DB3 at Lv15+ (2d8+6).

Weapon of Choice.effect: Choose a specific weapon type (longsword, unarmed, slingshot, etc.). Accuracy Check is 4. "Damage": DB2 (1d12+6), DB4 at Lv7+ (2d10+8), DB6 at Lv15+ (3d10+12). Can take multiple times for different weapons.

Enhanced Training.effect: Each of your Pokémon gains and additional 20% the amount of experience they would gain normally.

Improved Attacks.trigger: Your Pokémon’s damage dealing attack hits.
Improved Attacks.effect: When adding up damage dealt to the foe, add either half of your ATK modifier or half of your SATK modifier before your foes subtracts their Defense or Special Defense from the attack.

Affirmation.effect: Whenever your Pokémon fells a foe or scores a critical hit, your Pokémon gains temporary Hit Points equal to twice your ATK modifier.

Beast Master.effect: Your Pokémon do not protest your commands. They cannot act disobedient unless they are Legendary. This doesn’t affect their loyalty, only whether or not they do what you say to do.

Break Through!.trigger: Your Pokémon’s damage dealing attack hits.
Break Through!.effect: Your Pokémon’s attack deals damage without applying weaknesses, resistances, or immunities.

Brutal Workout.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Brutal Workout.target: Your Pokémon who just defeated another Pokémon.
Brutal Workout.effect: The target loses half of its current HP but gains +100% the experience they would have gained from the foe they just felled.

Constructive Criticism.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Constructive Criticism.target: Your Pokémon that missed all targets with a move on its previous turn.
Constructive Criticism.effect: Your Pokémon needs to roll -3 on accuracy checks for the remainder of the encounter.

Focus.frequency: Daily – Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Focus.effect: The next time your Pokémon uses a Move without a Damage Dice Roll, subtract half your SATK modifier from the Accuracy Check. This may not be used with moves that set the target’s HP to 0.

Improved Attacks +.trigger: Your Pokémon’s damage dealing attack hits.
Improved Attacks +.effect: When adding up damage dealt to the foe, add your ATK modifier or your SATK modifier before your foes subtracts their Defense or Special Defense from the attack. This Feature replaces Improved Attacks.

Improved Attacks Z.prerequisites: Ace Trainer, Improved Attacks +, 18 ATK, 18 SATK
Improved Attacks Z.trigger: Your Pokémon’s damage dealing attack hits.
Improved Attacks Z.effect: When adding up damage dealt to the foe, add your ATK modifier and your SATK modifier before your foes subtracts their Defense or Special Defense from the attack. The Feature replaces Improved Attacks +.

Intimidate.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Intimidate.effect: You use the Intimidate Ability on the target.

Press.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Press.effect: Deal damage to your Pokémon equal to 1/5th of their total HP. Raise any of their stats’ Combat Stages 1 level. Using Press more than once per Pokémon, per day will affect their loyalty.

Press +.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Press +.effect: Deal damage to your Pokémon equal to 1/4th of their total HP with your weapon. Raise any of their stats’ Combat Stages 2 levels. Using Press + more than once per Pokémon, per day will affect their loyalty.

Taskmaster.effect: Whenever you use Press, Press+, or Positive Press on a target, remove the Flinched and Confused conditions on the target if applicable, and raise any negative combat stages to 0.

No Escape.effect: When Pokémon or trainers try to flee during an encounter that you are participating in and their movement speed is equal to or greater than yours or your Pokémon’s, they must roll 1d20 and add either their SPD modifier or half their relevant Speed Skill. If they surpass 10 + your ATK modifier, they successfully escape. Otherwise, they are unable to leave the encounter. Someone using the Run Away ability does not need to roll to escape regardless of their movement speed.

Torrential Assault.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Torrential Assault.trigger: A Pokémon or trainer successfully flees or a trainer recalls their Pokémon.
Torrential Assault.effect: You may have an active Pokémon make an attack on a Pokémon or trainer as an Interrupt before they escape or are returned. If you are attacking a Pokémon that has fainted and you lower them to -100% they do not need to make a death savings throw.

Aha! Got you!.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Aha! Got you!.effect: When your Pokémon use a move with the Trap keyword, you may choose to maximize its duration instead of rolling.

Bloodthirst.effect: You can tell if a target is under 50% HP. In addition to this, whenever a hostile target is below 50% HP, your active Pokémon gains 1 Speed Combat Stage. This Speed Combat Stage fades when no hostile targets are below 50% health, and your Pokémon do not gain multiple Combat Stages when multiple enemies are under 50% HP.

Don\u2019t Stop.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Don\u2019t Stop.trigger: Your Pokémon knocks out a foe.
Don\u2019t Stop.effect: After knocking out a foe, your Pokémon may make another Shift and use another Move in that same turn. You may only activate this feature once per Pokémon per encounter.

Finish Them!.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Finish Them!.target: Your Pokémon using a damaging Move.
Finish Them!.effect: When your Pokémon hits with Move with a Damage Dice Roll. If the Move’s target has less than 5% HP after dealing damage, the Pokémon faints.

Hunting Techniques.effect: After ten minutes of work, roll 1d20 and add your ATK modifier. If you roll 15 or higher, that Pokémon learns the Move Mean Look, Odor Sleuth, or Pursuit. You must own a Pokémon that already knows whichever Move you are tutoring onto the target.

Natural High.frequency: Daily—Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Natural High.effect: After knocking out a foe, chose a stat other than HP. That stat is raised 1 Combat Stage.

No Escape +.effect: When Pokémon or trainers try to flee during an encounter that you are participating in and their movement speed is equal to or greater than yours or your Pokémon’s, they must roll 1d20 and add either their SPD modifier or half their relevant Speed Skill. If they surpass 15 + your ATK modifier, they successfully escape. Otherwise, they are unable to leave the encounter. Someone using the Run Away ability must roll to escape as if they did not have that ability. If the target successfully escapes, your movement speed as well as the movement speed of all your Pokémon increases by 4 meters per round for 10 minutes if you try to pursue the escapee. Replaces No Escape.

Pursuit.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day

Shifting Pursuit.effect: When you get this feature, choose a type. When you or one of your Pokémon uses the Move Pursuit, Pursuit may instead be that chosen type instead of Dark. You may not change the Type once you pick the type. You may take this Feature more than once to acquire multiple elemental types for Pursuit.

Sprints.prerequisites: Chaser, 16 SATK, a Pokémon with a Speed stat of 20 or greater
Sprints.effect: You may add to your Pokémon’s Speed stat during level up and ignore Base Relation, but only for the Speed Stat.

Thrill of the Hunt.effect: When your Pokémon pursues a foe and fells them, with Pursuit, because of a Chaser feature, or in an encounter after the target has previously escaped, they gain +25% the experience they would have gained.

Boundless Endurance.effect: You may add to your Pokémon’s HP stat when they level up, ignoring Base Relation.

Press On!.effect: Your Pokémon faint when they reach -25% HP instead of at 0 HP. Pokémon cannot use the Moves Endeavor, Explosion, Flail, Pain Split, Reversal or Self-destruct while they have 0 HP or less. You may still return a Pokémon with 0 or less HP and send out a Pokémon in the same action. Pokémon with the Soulless ability cannot benefit from Press On! This does not change when your Pokémon need to make death savings throws.

Aware.effect: You may sleep and have just as much awareness to sound, feeling, and smell as if you are awake. You also instinctively sense danger while asleep and can wake up instantly. If you have the status affliction Sleep, treat it as normal non-status afflicted sleep. You do not need to make perception checks with a penalty while asleep.

Hold!.prerequisites: Enduring Soul, 3 Pokémon with a Defense stat of 25 or more
Hold!.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Hold!.trigger: You are hit by a Move using the Attack stat or you are hit by a Trainer Arms Feature attack using Attack.
Hold!.effect: Lose 25 HP instead of the damage you would have taken.

Padding.effect: Subtract your ATK modifier from the damage you would take in addition to your Defense or Special Defense stat.

Padding +.trigger: You are hit by a Move.
Padding +.effect: Subtract twice your ATK modifier from the damage you would take in addition to your Defense or Special Defense stat. This Feature replaces Padding.

Soul\u2019s Protection.prerequisites: Enduring Soul, a Pokémon with the Move Protect
Soul\u2019s Protection.frequency: Daily – Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Soul\u2019s Protection.trigger: You are hit by a Move.

Soul\u2019s Endurance.prerequisites: Enduring Soul, a Pokémon with the Move Endure
Soul\u2019s Endurance.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Soul\u2019s Endurance.trigger: You are hit by a Move.

Split.frequency: Daily – Every 7 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Split.trigger: One of your adjacent Pokémon is hit by a damaging Move.
Split.effect: After applying Defense and Special Defense, you take half of the damage that would have been done to your Pokémon. Your Pokémon takes half of the damage it was supposed to take.

Stand!.prerequisites: Enduring Soul, 3 Pokémon with a Special Defense stat of 25 or more
Stand!.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Stand!.trigger: You are hit by a Move using the Special Attack stat or you are hit by a Trainer Arms Feature attack using special Attack.
Stand!.effect: Lose 25 HP instead of the damage you would have taken.

Still Standing.trigger: You are lowered to or below 0 HP.
Still Standing.effect: You may still issue commands to your Pokémon while unconscious. If you chose to, and in that encounter, you are lowered to -100% HP, you may not make a death saving roll.

Still Standing +.trigger: You are lowered to or below 0 HP.
Still Standing +.effect: You may still issue commands to your Pokémon while unconscious. You may still make a death savings throw when lowered to -100% HP. This Feature replaces Still Standing.

Specialist Training.target: The stat of choice for any of your Pokémon.
Specialist Training.effect: You may add half of your ATK or SATK modifier to the Base stat chosen for Stat Ace to your Pokémon. If this changes which of the Pokémon’s base stats are the highest, maintain Base Relation appropriately.

Stat Subversion.effect: That Pokémon’s stat is decreased by one Combat Stage according to whichever Stat you’ve chosen for Stat Ace. You may only effect one target with Stat Subversion at a time, you may not change targets until the original is unable to battle. When you take Stat Ace, choose Attack, Defense, Special Attack, Special Defense, or Speed. This becomes your chosen stat for Stat Ace. You may take Stat Ace multiple times, choosing different stats each time.

Fixed Competence.effect: Your Pokémon may not lose Combat Stages in your chosen Stat.

Positive Press.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Positive Press.effect: Deal damage to your Pokémon equal to 1/5th of their total HP with your weapon. Raise your chosen stats Combat Stage 2 levels.

Specialist Training+.prerequisites: Stat Ace, Specialist Training, 3 Badges and/or Medals
Specialist Training+.effect: You may add your ATK or SATK modifier to your chosen stat in the base stat of every Pokémon you own. For every 10 levels your Pokémon have, they gain 1 extra point in your chosen Stat. If this changes which of the Pokémon’s base stats are the highest, maintain Base Relation appropriately.

Stat Boost.effect: For 1 encounter the target gains the Ability Speed Boost. If your chosen Stat is not Speed, replace the Ability’s name with Attack Boost, Special Attack Boost, Defense Boost or Special Defense Boost appropriately. Also if your chosen Stat is not Speed, replace the word Speed in Speed Boost’s effect with Attack, Special Attack, Defense or Special Defense appropriately.

Stat Overflow.prerequisites: Stat Ace, Press + or Positive Press
Stat Overflow.effect: Your Pokémon loses half of its full HP. The Pokémon’s Stat, chosen for Stat Ace, is set to +6 Combat Stages. Your Pokémon cannot use a Move during this round of encounter but may still shift.

Stat Subversion +.target: Enemy Pokémon’s stat you chose Stat Ace for.
Stat Subversion +.effect: That Pokémon’s stat is decreased by two Combat Stages according to whichever Stat you’ve chosen for Stat Ace. You may only effect one target with Stat Subversion at a time, you may not change targets until the original is unable to battle. This Feature replaces Stat Subversion.

Stat Unlock.prerequisites: Stat Ace, a Pokémon with 30 in your chosen Stat
Stat Unlock.effect: Your Pokémon ignore Base Relation, if they are adding to the Stat chosen for Stat Ace.

Superior Ability.frequency: Daily – Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Superior Ability.effect: If your active Pokémon’s Stat, chosen for Stat Ace, is higher than the target’s particular stat, that Stat is lowered 1 Combat Stage.

Talent Scout.prerequisites: Stat Ace, 2 Pokémon with Natures that add to chosen Stat
Talent Scout.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Talent Scout.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your ATK modifier. If the total exceeds 15, that Pokémon’s Nature becomes a random Nature that benefits your chosen Stat. If the total exceeds 25, that Pokémon has the Nature of your choice, that benefits your chosen stat.

Field Scout.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Field Scout.effect: On its next turn, the target may use the move Rapid Spin as if it was on their Move List, and deals additional damage equal to your ATK modifier.

Terrain Mastery.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Terrain Mastery.effect: For the remainder of the encounter, none of your Pokémon receive movement penalties for moving through Rough Terrain, nor Accuracy Penalties when targeting foes in Rough Terrain.

Adaptive Boost.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Adaptive Boost.trigger: Your Pokémon uses a Self-Targeting move that raises Combat Stages.
Adaptive Boost.effect: Your Pokémon gains an additional combat stage in any Stat in which they didn’t gain a combat stage that turn.

Heightened Potential.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Heightened Potential.trigger: Your Pokémon makes a check on a 1d20 to use a Skill Effect: Add your ATK modifier to the 1d20 result.
Heightened Potential.effect: Add your ATK modifier to the 1d20 result. This may be used to modify an Accuracy Roll when using the Threaded Skill.

Helpful Priorities.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Helpful Priorities.trigger: Your Pokémon uses a Move that targets only other allies.
Helpful Priorities.effect: Your Pokémon gains X HP, where X is your SATK Modifier doubled. Helpful Priorities may be triggered on Burst moves like Heal Bell that target Self as well, but only if they affect an Allied Pokémon.

Hazardous Intent.effect: When your Pokémon are placing Hazards, your Pokémon may place an additional X meters of Hazards, where X is your ATK modifier. You may place Hazards as you like within your range, and each meter of hazards need not be adjacent to another. If you put Hazards in a square occupied by an enemy, they immediately suffer the effects of the Hazard upon Shifting.

Move Recognition.effect: When your opponent’s Pokémon uses a Move that it has already used in that encounter, it must roll 4 higher on Accuracy Check to hit your Pokémon.

Personal Walls.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Personal Walls.trigger: Your Pokémon uses a Move with the Wall Keyword.
Personal Walls.effect: When using moves with the Wall keyword, instead of creating a Wall, your Pokémon creates a Coat with the wall’s effect. This effect has a single target, and your Pokémon may target itself or others with this effect. These Coats last until the end of the encounter, or until the target is recalled.

Quick Set-Up.trigger: Your Pokémon uses a move with the Coat, Hazard, Wall, or Weather keyword, or uses a Skill to affect Terrain.
Quick Set-Up.effect: Your Pokémon may immediately use a different move with the Coat, Hazard, Wall, or Weather Keyword, or use a different Skill to affect Terrain.

Terrain Tactics.effect: Your Pokémon with the Fountain, Freezer, Groundshaper, Materializer, or Sprouter Skills may, instead of performing a Move on its turn, target up to X square meters within 6 meters, turning the targeted spaces into Rough Terrain. X is equal to half your ATK Modifier plus the Pokémon’s Power or Intelligence Skill, whichever is higher. This ability may be limited by the targeted terrain; for example, while a Pokémon with Fountain could turn dirt into mud to create rough terrain, it may not be able to create rough terrain when fighting on concrete.

Versatility.effect: The target may forfeit the use of a Battle Frequency Move to regain use of a different Battle Frequency Move, or they may forfeit the use of a Center Frequency Move to regain the use of a different Battle or Center Frequency Move from their Move List.

Weather Vortex.frequency: Daily – Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Weather Vortex.trigger: Your Pokémon uses a move with the Weather keyword Effect: The target’s Weather does not affect the entire field, and does not replace any existing Weather conditions.
Weather Vortex.effect: The target’s Weather does not affect the entire field, and does not replace any existing Weather conditions. Instead, your Pokémon targets a space within 6 meters of itself. From that space originates a Blast with a radius of up to 4 meters, though it may be smaller if you wish. Your Weather Move affects only this area, and acts as normal within this area.

Type Training.trigger: Your chosen type Pokémon gains experience points.
Type Training.effect: Each of your chosen type Pokémon gains +30% the amount of experience they would gain normally.

Improved Type Attacks.trigger: Your Pokémon’s chosen type damage dealing attack hits.
Improved Type Attacks.effect: When adding up damage dealt to the foe, add half of your ATK modifier to the total. You may take Type Ace multiple times. Each time, you must choose different types.

Elemental Shifting.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Elemental Shifting.effect: X is your ATK modifier multiplied by 6 seconds, or your ATK modifier multiplied by 1 round. The target temporarily gains your chosen Type. If it has 2 Types already, temporarily replace one of their types. It becomes that Type for X. At the end of combat apply experience multipliers from Type Training.

Elemental Sync.effect: Elemental Sync takes approximately 10 hours to complete. The targeted Pokémon permanently gains your chosen Type. If the target has two Types, permanently replace one Type. If this Pokémon is a single type, it gains a second type. The Pokémon’s physical appearance is altered appropriately. You may take this feature up to three times.

Improved Type Attacks +.trigger: Your Pokémon’s chosen type damage dealing attack hits.
Improved Type Attacks +.effect: When adding up damage dealt to the foe, add your ATK modifier to the total. This feature replaces Improved Type Attacks.

Move Shift.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Move Shift.effect: The Move’s Type temporarily becomes your chosen Type for that use.

Move Sync.effect: Move Sync takes approximately 5 hours to complete. The targeted move is now permanently your chosen Type.

Soulbound.effect: When taking damage of your chosen type, treat yourself as if you are Resistant.

Superior Typing.target: Your Pokémon of your chosen type.
Superior Typing.effect: When your foe uses attacks of your chosen type they do not add STAB. When your foe uses Moves of your chosen type they cannot Critical Hit or cause Death Savings Thows to your chosen typed Pokémon with those Moves.

Type Soul.effect: You feel obligated to use your chosen Type; at any time you should have at least four Pokémon with your chosen Type . You can sense Pokémon of that type nearby; up to 5-meters multiplied by your SATK modifier away. You can also sense that Type in Move lists of Pokémon; up to 5-meters multiplied by your SATK modifier away.

Type Connection.target: An indifferent or hostile Wild Pokémon of your chosen type
Type Connection.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your ATK modifier. If the result is 15 or higher, the Pokémon becomes more friendly. Targets that were initially indifferent may show interest in the trainer. Targets that were initially hostile may still be wary, or may decide to flee, but will cease attacking. Targets may become hostile again if you attack it or its allies. You may target any single Pokémon with this feature only once per day.

With The Elements.trigger: You start an encounter with wild Pokémon.
With The Elements.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SATK modifier. If the result is 15 or higher, if the area contains a wild Pokémon of your chosen type, at least one wild Pokémon of that type appears at the encounter.

Egg Factory.effect: You may make up to 7 different Breeding Checks per day, and the check for Breeding is 25 instead of 15. Subtract your DEF or SDEF mod from the breeding check. Your Pokémon must still have at least 6 hours together, instead of 8. You can identify what Pokémon will hatch from an Egg.

Natural Edge.effect: Add half of your DEF modifier to any of the Pokémon’s base stat and add half of your SDEF modifier to any different base stat of the same Pokémon. This becomes your Pokémon’s new base stats. A Pokémon may only have one Natural Edge or Natural Edge + applied to it when it hatches. This bonus may not exceed +6 in either stat.

Breed.target: Two Pokémon who are compatible for breeding.
Breed.effect: Give the targets at least 8 hours of time alone, they will be guaranteed to produce an egg. Egg Hatcher Prerequisites: Breeder Static Target: Eggs in the possession of the Breeder. Effect: Eggs hatch in 80% of the time it usually would.

Egg Hatcher +.target: Eggs in the possession of the Breeder.
Egg Hatcher +.effect: Eggs hatch in 70% of the time it usually would. The Feature replaces Egg Hatcher.

Head Start.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF mod. If the result is 12 or greater, the Pokémon learns all moves learned at or before level “X” where X equals your Special Defense stat.

Home Cooking.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Home Cooking.effect: After 1 hour of work, roll 1d4 and add your SDEF modifier. The total represents the amount of food portions you make for humans and or Pokémon, feeding them for the whole day. Pokémon that eat your food have their loyalty towards you slightly increased. You know what dietary needs a Pokémon has.

I\u2019ll Take Good Care of It.target: A non-hostile Female Wild Pokémon with eggs.
I\u2019ll Take Good Care of It.effect: Roll 1d100 and subtract your DEF modifier. If you rolled lower than the target’s base capture rate, you may receive an egg from the target Pokémon. This roll can be modified by your GM depending on its mood towards you. If you fail, the target may become hostile.

Latent Potential.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Latent Potential.target: A Pokémon who is levelling up to a level evenly divisible by 5.
Latent Potential.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF Modifier. If the total exceeds 15, the Pokémon learns any Move on its level-up list from any levels lower than its current level.

Litters.prerequisites: Breeder, a Pokémon who has helped to produce at least 2 eggs Weekly
Litters.effect: Roll 1d4. The roll represents how many eggs are produced after breeding if the breeding was successful. This number may not be modified.

Litters +.effect: Roll 1d6 and add 1. The roll represents how many eggs are produced after breeding if the breeding was successful. This number may not be modified.

Natural Edge +.effect: Add your DEF modifier to any of the Pokémon’s base stat and add your SDEF modifier to any different base stat of the same Pokémon. This becomes your Pokémon’s new base stats. Neither bonus may exceed +6. This Feature replaces Natural Edge.

Natural Progression.trigger: A Pokémon you own levels up to a level evenly divisible by 5 Effect: The target Pokémon gains +1 to two different base stats.
Natural Progression.effect: The target Pokémon gains +1 to two different base stats. The chosen stats must remain the same each time this feature is applied per Pokémon, and the cumulative bonus cannot exceed the +6. When this is used on a Pokémon who has had Natural Edge/+ applied, the same stats must be chosen and the combination of the two feats still may not exceed the +6 limits. This feature is not retroactive.

Never Would Have Happened.target: 2 opposite gendered Pokémon who aren’t in the same egg group.
Never Would Have Happened.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your DEF and SDEF modifiers. If the total is higher than 22, they are allowed to breed but must still make a Breeding Check to see if an egg is produced.

Tender Loving Care.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Tender Loving Care.effect: After four hours of work, heal a number of Pokémon equal to your DEF or SDEF modifier to their full HP and cure them of any status afflictions. Center Frequency moves are not restored by Tender Loving Care.

Berry Planter.effect: You create a portable Planter, able to grow and produce plants which grow Apricorns or Berries. Plant an Apricorn or Berry into the Planter Extension, roll 1d4 and add 1. The berry plant will produce Berries in that many days. Once ready to pick, roll 1d4 to determine how many Berries grow per plant. You may plant up to 2 Berries/Apricorns in your planter. You may not plant a third Berry/ Apricorn until you harvest Berries and make room for it. You may make the Planter out of whatever materials you’d like. The Planter should be about 15 lbs./7kg in weight and at least 6x12x18 in./0.15x0.3x0.45m in size.

Healer Berry Search.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day
Healer Berry Search.target: A Route, or any wilderness area with plant life.
Healer Berry Search.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. On a result of 15 or higher, you find a berry. To determine its type, roll 1d10. The result is the ID number of the berry you find in the berry index.

Altering Berry Search.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day
Altering Berry Search.target: A Route, or any wilderness area with plant life.
Altering Berry Search.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier, and choose a Pokémon that you own with a stat above 20. On a result of 20 or higher, you find a berry that lowers the stat you selected.

Berry Efficiency.effect: When using any given berry, the target may not consume it if you choose, but still benefits from its effect. The second time the target benefits from the effects of the same berry, it is consumed as normal. If applied to a reusable berry, this feature allows use of the berry twice in a day without becoming useless.

Berry Expert.effect: You can identify any Berry, its properties, and what it yields in terms of Contest Stats. When you are making Berries into Poffins, Aprijuice, or PokeBlocks, you get +2 to your rolls. When your Pokémon eat a flavor of Berry that they like, any effects of any loyalty gained is doubled.

Berry Merge.effect: Choose 5 berries with an identical set of effects. The berries are consumed, and you create a hold item that has the same effects as the component berries, but is not consumed on use. If the item is used more than once a day, it breaks and becomes useless. Call the item whatever you’d like. The new item is no longer a berry.

Booster Berry Search.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day
Booster Berry Search.target: A Route, or any wilderness area with plant life.
Booster Berry Search.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier, and choose one of your Pokémon with a stat of 20 or higher. On a result of 15 or higher, you find a berry that increases a Combat Stage when below 50% HP. The stat increased by the berry will be the stat you chose when selecting a Pokémon.

Eat It!.frequency: Daily - Every 7 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day
Eat It!.effect: The target immediately consumes and benefits from the effects of what berry they are holding, regardless of their current HP or status.

Effectiveness Berry Search.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day
Effectiveness Berry Search.target: A Route, or any wilderness area with plant life.
Effectiveness Berry Search.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier, and optionally choose a Pokémon that you own. On a result of 20 or higher, you find a berry that reduces damage from a type that your chosen Pokémon is weak to. If you do not choose a Pokémon when using this feature, you find a Chilan Berry instead.

Flavor Berry Search.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day
Flavor Berry Search.target: A Route, or any wilderness area with plant life.
Flavor Berry Search.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. On a result of 15 or higher, you find a berry. To determine its type, roll 1d6+10 (reroll a result of 16). The result is the ID number of the Berry you find in the berry listing.

Hybridization.effect: When planting a berry, you may instead plant two berries in the same spot, using parts of each and being unable to use the remainder. The resulting berry plant will grow berries that are a combination of both the ingredients, and has both their effects. When eaten, if both effects cannot apply, then only apply the relevant one. Berries created as a result of Hybridization cannot be used again for Hybridization.

Plain Berry Search.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day
Plain Berry Search.target: A Route, or any wilderness area with plant life.
Plain Berry Search.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. On a result of 15 or higher, you find a berry that has no effect of your choice.

Rare Berry Search.prerequisites: Botanist, has grown a Starf, Enigma, Micle, Custap, Jaboca or Rowap Berry
Rare Berry Search.target: A Route, or any wilderness area with plant life.
Rare Berry Search.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. On a result of 20 or higher, you find a berry. To determine its type, roll 1d6+58. The result is the number of the berry you find in the berry listing.

Special Nutrients.effect: Choose a planted berry. The berry will grow to full size and bear fruit within 24-X hours, where X is your SDEF modifier.

Energy Boost.effect: After working for fifteen minutes, you create an Energy Drink that provides Trainers with Temporary Hit-Points equal to your DEF modifier x 5. The Temporary Hit-Points disappear in 1 hour.

Soul Food.effect: After working for thirty minutes, create a Dish for up to 10 people and/or Pokémon from whatever edible food you’d like. Pokémon that eat this meal will have their mood greatly increased, and will not lose loyalty if they consume Herbal Medicines shortly before or after. Many Chef Features are Extended Actions. They need to be prepared as one would prepare a meal, so keep this in mind to know how long you would take to use a particular Feature with your Trainer based on their skills and available equipment.

Apricorn Blender.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Apricorn Blender.effect: Add your DEF modifier to the value of the Aprijuice.

Baby Food.frequency: Daily – Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Baby Food.effect: After working for thirty minutes, you create a Dish that, when eaten by a Pokémon of level 25 or lower, causes that Pokémon to gain an additional 25% the amount of experience they would gain normally for the rest of the day.

Bait.effect: After working for one hour, create a Dish and place it somewhere in the wilderness. Then, if you have sufficiently hidden yourself, after at least fifteen minutes, but no longer than one hour, you GM must have a wild Pokémon appear. Roll 1d20 and add your DEF modifier. If your roll is less than 10, a random Pokémon, based on your GM’s discretion will appear. If you roll 15 or higher a Pokémon of at least the same level as your highest levelled and loyal Pokémon will appear. If you roll 20 or higher, a Pokémon at least 5 levels higher than your highest levelled and loyal Pokémon will appear. Pokémon acquired through the use of Bait cannot be used as the basis of determining the level of other Pokémon lured by using Bait.

Herbal Medicine.effect: After working for one hour Create a Heal Powder or Energy Powder.

Poffin Mixer.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Poffin Mixer.target: A Poffin that is being mixed.
Poffin Mixer.effect: Add your DEF modifier to the value of the Poffin or Pokeblock.

Soldier Pills.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Soldier Pills.effect: After working for one hour you create a Soldier Pill that provides Trainers with enough energy to go 1 day without sleep. After 48 hours without sleep, the item’s consumer loses 10 HP for every hour without sleep. While under the effects of a Soldier Pill, you may not be given the Sleep Status Affliction. If you would be given the Sleep Status Affliction, the Trainer loses 2 of the 48 hours allotted by Soldier Pills.

Sugar Rush.effect: After working for fifteen minutes, you create a Dish that provides Pokémon with Temporary Hit-Points equal to your DEF modifier x 2. The Temporary Hit-Points disappear in 1 hour.

Throw Bait.effect: Provided you have spent fifteen minutes earlier in the day to create it, you throw a Tasty Morsel for a wild Pokémon. Roll 1d20 and add your DEF modifier. If the result is 13 or higher, the Pokémon gives up its next action to sample the food. If the result is 25 or higher, the Pokémon gives up its next 2 actions to completely eat the food.

Vile Cook.target: A Dish or Tasty Morsel, you’ve just created
Vile Cook.effect: Choose Paralysis, Poison, or Sleep. Whenever the targeted Dish is consumed, it inflicts the chosen status.

Vitamins.effect: After working for three hours, roll 1d6. On a result of 1, you create an HP Up; on a result of 2, you create a Protein; on a result of 3, you create an Iron; on a result of 4, you create a Calcium; on a result of 5, you create a Zinc; on a result of 6, you create a Carbos.

Vitamins +.effect: After working for three hours, create a HP Up, a Protein, an Iron, a Calcium, a Zinc or a Carbos. This feature replaces Vitamins.

Good Grooming.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Good Grooming.effect: After a 10-minute grooming, the target is temporarily at full Loyalty and appears more appealing. If they level up without a negative incident within 10 minutes of Good Grooming and have met all previous requirements for evolution, evolution is guaranteed. Loyalty returns to its usual level after 1 hour; repeated use of Good Grooming gradually raises its usual level of Loyalty. Good Grooming also cures Burns, Confusion, and Paralysis.

Contest Coating.effect: Your Pokémon may have up to 15 points in any Contest Stat. When participating in a Contest, you may burn one extra point of Contest appeal than you would usually be able to whenever you would be able to burn a point.

Agility Training.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Agility Training.effect: After a 10-minute Grooming, the target’s Speed Skills are +3 for the next 24 hours.

Blinding Coat.frequency: Daily – Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Blinding Coat.effect: After a 10-minute Grooming, roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total is 15 or higher, any time an enemy Pokémon or Trainer would target the Pokémon for the next 24 hours, they must roll +5 in order to hit during Accuracy. This may not target the same Pokémon more than once per day.

Contest Breeding.effect: Choose a Contest Stat from one of the Egg’s parents. The Egg hatches with half of the value of the chosen Contest Stat.

Perfect Contest Breeding.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Perfect Contest Breeding.effect: Choose a Contest Stat from one of the Egg’s parents, and then choose a different Contest Stat from the Egg’s other parent. The Egg hatches with either the full value of one of the parents’ Contest Stats or hatches with half of each of the Egg’s parents’ Contest Stats. This Feature replaces Contest Breeding.

Rising Star.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Rising Star.target: A Pokémon who is levelling up to a level evenly divisible by 5.
Rising Star.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total is 10 or higher, choose a Contest Stat that the target already has at least 1 point in, and add 2 more points to the Stat. Rising Star can’t raise any specific Contest Stat above 15.

Sheen Breeding.target: Pokémon who are breeding and have successfully produced an egg.
Sheen Breeding.effect: Roll 1d100 and subtract your DEF and SDEF modifier doubled from the result. If you rolled 0 or under, when the egg hatches, the Pokémon will be Shiny.

Sign Training.effect: Your Pokémon under the effects of Agility Training can use Moves and Shift on your command through non-verbal communication. This could be hand signals, audible cues, or anything that you work out that still requires some action on your part for your Pokémon to interpret.

Specs Grooming.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Specs Grooming.effect: After a 10-minute Grooming, at the start of the target’s next encounter, raise the target’s X Combat Stage 1 level. X is Attack, Defense, Special Attack, Special Defense or Speed, but only if the target has a corresponding Contest Ribbon. For X, Beauty Ribbon allows Special Attack; Cool Ribbon allows Attack; Cute Ribbon allows Speed; Tough Ribbon allows Defense or Smart Ribbon allows Special Defense. When using Specs Grooming, choose only one X at a time per use. You cannot target the same Pokémon more than once per day with Specs Grooming.

Specs Grooming +.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Specs Grooming +.effect: After a 15-minute Grooming, at the start of the target’s next encounter, raise the target’s X Combat Stage 2 levels. X is Attack, Defense, Special Attack, Special Defense or Speed, but only if the target has a corresponding Contest Ribbon. For X, Beauty Ribbon allows Special Attack; Cool Ribbon allows Attack; Cute Ribbon allows Speed; Tough Ribbon allows Defense or Smart Ribbon allows Special Defense. When using Specs Grooming +, choose only one X at a time per use. You cannot target the same Pokémon more than once per day with Specs Grooming +. This Feature replaces Specs Grooming.

You Get It from Me.effect: Whenever an Egg hatches, add your SDEF or DEF modifier to any of the Egg’s Contest Stats if you’ve won at least 1 Ribbon for the Contest type you choose.

This Is How You Do It, Kid.target: A Pokémon you’ve hatched, within at least 20 meters of one of its parents.
This Is How You Do It, Kid.effect: When the parent Pokémon gains Experience Points, instead it gains half of the experience points it would have gained, then the target gains the other half of the experience points. If both parents of a particular target are nearby, you may still only use This Is How You Do It, Kid’s Effect once.

Egg Move Advantage.effect: Your SDEF modifier halved is X. The hatching Pokémon learns up to X of their Egg Moves, as long as either parents have the Move(s) on their Level Up or Tutor Move list. These Moves are decided at random. You can only have one instance of any attack. If a Pokémon has fewer legal Level Up/ Tutor Moves than X, they learn all of the possible Moves.

Attitude Blinders.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Attitude Blinders.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the result is 16 or higher, choose 6 Natures and assign each to the numbers 2, 3, 4, 5, 6. Roll 1d6; on the result, from 1 to 6, give the hatching egg the assigned Nature.

Attitude Blinders +.frequency: Daily - Every 10 Levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Attitude Blinders +.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the result is 16 or higher, choose a nature and give the hatching Pokémon the assigned Nature. This Feature replaces Attitude Blinders.

Birthright.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Birthright.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the result is higher than 15, the Pokémon hatches with the Ability of your choice.

Born Strong.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Born Strong.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the result is 13 or higher, the Pokémon hatches with +1 to all of its Speed Skills and +1 to its Power Skill. If it evolves, these bonuses carry over to its new stage. This Feature cannot raise a Power Skill to 8 or higher.

Egg Move Tutor.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the result is higher than 23, the target can be taught an Egg Move it has not yet learned. Egg Move Tutor can only successfully target the same Pokémon 4 times.

Master Hatcher.target: Eggs in the possession of the Breeder.
Master Hatcher.effect: Eggs hatch in 50% of the time it would usually take. This Feature replaces Egg Hatcher or Egg Hatcher +.

Natural Move Economy.effect: Egg Moves and Level Up Moves may be placed in your Pokémon’s “TM/Tutor Move” Move Slots. Moves taught by TMs or Tutors, which appear on your Pokémon’s Egg Move List or Natural Move List, may be placed on you Pokémon’s “Level Up/Egg Move” List.

Not Yet, Little One.effect: If an Egg that you, or one of your allied Trainers, own is starting to hatch, you may prevent it from hatching for up to 48 hours. If you do not own the Egg, you need permission from its owner to prevent it from hatching.

Parental Hyperlexia.effect: You understand your Pokémon above Level 15 that you have hatched as if they are speaking to you in your language. They are equally capable of understanding you through body language as if you were verbally commanding them.

Philoprogenitive.effect: If any Pokémon, within your Shift value, that you originally hatched has been successfully hit by a Move or Feature you may Shift as a Free Action to Intercept that Move. You take the damage and effects of that Move or Feature, after subtracting X from the incoming damage, where X is your DEF and SDEF modifier combined. You may only use Philoprogenitive once per encounter.

Thoroughbred.effect: Any Pokémon you hatch, that were also hatched from at least one Pokémon you own and are “Thoroughbred.” Your Thoroughbred Pokémon gain +50% the amount of experience they would gain normally.

Treat Minor Wounds.frequency: Daily – Every 7 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
