Frantic Research.effect: Roll d20 and add your SATK and SDEF modifiers to the roll. If you roll higher then 15, you learn what level the Pokémon is. If you roll higher then 20, you identify how much HP the target currently has.

Walking Encyclopedia.target: Anything related to knowledge you can look up.
Walking Encyclopedia.effect: You know Pokémon data, relating to Pokémon in your Pokédex, about move sets, type, type advantage, evolution methods, abilities, height, weight, and location. You have access to all information a Pokédex would grant, immediately.

Did the Homework.effect: Spend an hour doing research about the target. Every 12 levels gained, you may research an additional target during this hour. The GM gives you X Insight Points for the target that last until the end of the day, where X is your SATK or SDEF modifier. While near the target and taking actions related to that target for the rest of the day, you may spend an Insight Point once per round as a free action to treat either your SATK or SDEF mod as 3 higher for the duration of that round. When you spend an Insight Point, your GM gives you a fact about the target.

Field Commander.effect: You may issue commands to Pokémon who has not already acted on a round of an encounter. There must be understood consent from the Pokémon’s trainer unless they are unconscious. Pokémon may only act once per round of an encounter. You still may only issue one command per round unless you have Dual Wielding.

Frantic Research +.frequency: Daily – Every 3 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day
Frantic Research +.effect: Roll d20 and add your SATK and SDEF modifiers to the roll. If you roll higher then 15, you learn what level the Pokémon is and at least 6 of its attacks that a standard one of its species would not naturally have at its level. If it has fewer than 6 attacks meeting this criterion, you learn all of them. If you roll higher then 20, you identify how much HP the target currently has. This Feature replaces Frantic Research.

Game Plan.frequency: Daily – Every 6 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day
Game Plan.effect: Shift the target up to X meters where X is your SATK modifier or your SDEF modifier.

Game Plan +.frequency: Daily – Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day
Game Plan +.effect: Shift all allies and their Pokémon up to X meters where X is your SATK modifier or your SDEF modifier. Game Plan + does not replace Game Plan.

Read the Manual.effect: You know the function of any machine. Additionally, you know the function of the item if it is a standard item with an effect given in the Player Handbook. You know of any defects that might change the way the item normally functions.

Repel Crafting.effect: After one hour of work, create a Repel. Name the concoction whatever you’d like out of whatever ingredients you’d like.

Strategic Targeting.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day
Strategic Targeting.effect: Add half your SDEF modifier to the target’s accuracy roll when they make an attack this turn.

Tactical Response.effect: Choose any Move and then pick one No Damage - Self Move known by the target. For the rest of the day when your targeted Pokémon is active and a foe uses the first Move chosen, it may immediately use the chosen No Damage - Self Move as an interrupt one time. This does not consume your command for the turn.

Trait Theory.effect: Roll d20 and add your SATK and SDEF modifiers to the roll. If you roll higher then 10, you identify what Nature the Pokémon has. If you roll higher than 15, you also identify what Abilities the Pokémon has.

Trait Theory +.effect: Roll d20 and add your SATK and SDEF modifiers to the roll. If you roll higher then 10, you identify what Nature the Pokémon has. If you roll higher than 15, you also identify what Abilities the Pokémon has. If the target is Wild and you rolled higher then 20, you may pick the target’s Nature. This Feature Replaces Trait Theory.

Praise.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Praise.effect: Roll d20 and add your SATK and SDEF modifiers to the roll. If you roll higher then 15, the target favors you and will hesitate to attack you and your Pokémon. The Legendary will also like you more.

Walking Mythos.target: Anything related to knowledge you can look up.
Walking Mythos.effect: You know Pokémon data, relating to Pokémon in your Pokédex, about move sets, type, type advantage, evolution methods, abilities, height, weight, and location. Restricted to Legendary Pokémon you have encountered.

Beckon.prerequisites: Cryptozoologist, used Praise on at least one Legendary
Beckon.target: A Legendary Pokémon’s habitat of a Legendary you’ve met.
Beckon.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 20, a Legendary Pokémon will appear. It will not necessarily appear near you, but you will be able to see it, or evidence of its presence.

Blessing.prerequisites: Cryptozoologist, allied with a Legendary Pokémon
Blessing.frequency: Daily – Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Blessing.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier doubled. The target recovers HP equal to the total rolled. You may not use Blessing if you are no longer allied with any Legendary Pokémon.

Channel Divinity.effect: The target may perform a non-Signature Move that is known by a Legendary Pokémon you are Linked with. This counts as Issuing a Command. This may only be used once per turn.

God Speak.prerequisites: Professor, Linguist, allied with a Legendary Pokémon
God Speak.effect: You can speak to and understand all Legendary Pokémon.

Guardian Link.prerequisites: Cryptozoologist, used Praise on at least one Legendary
Guardian Link.effect: You may only target Legendary Pokémon with Guardian Link if you are allied with them. The Legendary becomes Linked. While a Linked Legendary is in danger, regardless of where you are in the world, you will know.

Legendary Blessing.effect: Roll 2d20 and add your SDEF modifier tripled. The target recovers HP equal to the total rolled. You may not use Legendary Blessing if you are no longer allied with any Legendary Pokémon.

Link Summon.target: A Linked Legendary, disregarding any line of sight.
Link Summon.effect: You may summon the allied, Linked Legendary Pokémon. It will behave as it sees fit.

Miracle.trigger: Allied Trainer or Allied Pokémon fails a Death Savings Throw.
Miracle.effect: The target may re-roll that Death Savings throw and subtract 5 from their roll if you target a trainer or subtract 25 if you target a Pokémon.

Morale Blessing.prerequisites: Cryptozoologist, Field Commander, allied with a Legendary Pokémon
Morale Blessing.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day
Morale Blessing.effect: Pick either Attack, Defense, Special Attack, Special Defense or Speed and raise the chosen stat of each of your ally’s Pokémon’s within X meters of you by 1 Combat Stage until the end of the encounter. X is your SDEF modifier.

Omen.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day
Omen.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 10, you learn of the last time a Legendary Pokémon has been around the area.

Soul Searching.prerequisites: Cryptozoologist, Linked to a Legendary Pokémon
Soul Searching.target: A Linked Legendary, disregarding any line of sight.
Soul Searching.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 13, you are pointed in the direction of the target. If the total exceeds 17, you learn of the distance between the Linked Legendary and yourself.

Digging for the Past.effect: After thirty minutes of work, roll 1d20 and add your SATK modifier. If you roll higher than 15, you either find clues to fossils that are nearby or evidence of the area’s history. If you roll higher than 20 you choose to find either a piece of the area’s history or a fossil, both are chosen by your GM after you specify.

Stone Search.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Stone Search.effect: After thirty minutes of work, Roll 1d20 and add your SATK modifier. If you roll higher than 20, you find an Evolutionary stone chosen by your GM. Mechanics: See the Fossil Reanimation Rules determining how Fossils are reanimated.

Adventuring Archeologist.effect: You have begun to dabble in exploring ruins as well as caves. You know your regions history at a college level. You can sell or donate any pieces of history you find to museums while Digging for the Past for X, where X is 1d20+SATK mod times 30.

Dig, Dig, Dig, Dug.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Dig, Dig, Dig, Dug.effect: Over the course of five minutes you dig a 5-meter-wide and 2-meter-deep pit. The Pit Trap may be covered by camouflage that Pokémon or Trainers can’t distinguish from the surroundings. You can either choose to activate the trap from nearby or to have it activate when 100 pounds of pressure is put onto the camouflage.

Dust Settling.effect: After two minutes of work, modify your Pit Trap so that when something falls into your Pit Trap, use the Move SandAttack on the victim.

How to Ruin a Gneiss Day.frequency: Daily - Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
How to Ruin a Gneiss Day.effect: After ten minutes of work, modify your Pit Trap so that when something falls into your Pit Trap, use the Move Rock Slide on the victim.

Elemental Trapping.effect: After two minutes of work, graft an Evolution Stone into the Pit Trap. When the trap is activated use the Move Rock Slide, but instead of treating it as a Rock-Type Move, treat it like a move according to the type of Elemental Stone grafted onto the Pit Trap. Fire Stones deal Fire Type Damage; Leaf Stones, Grass; Thunder Stone, Electric; Water Stones, Water; Ice Stone, Ice; and Stones created through Stone Polishing do damage of their assigned type. The Stone is destroyed after use.

Movie Magic Quicksand.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Movie Magic Quicksand.effect: After ten minutes of work, modify your Pit Trap so that when something falls into your Pit Trap, use the Move Sand Tomb on the victim. The victims are stuck for 1d4 turns unless they have an Underground Speed Skill.

Reanimator.effect: After five hours of work, build a one-time use Reanimator Kit. You may use it immediately to make a Reanimation check. When you make a check to Reanimate a fossilized Pokémon add 3 to the roll. Reanimators can reanimate fossils anywhere with a steady power supply with their Reanimation Kit and do not need to visit a specialized lab. If the Kit is not used within 24 hours, it falls apart.

Slated for Renovation.effect: After three hours of work, while underground, you set off a controlled explosion. You may set off the explosion from up to 30 meters away. If anyone is within 5 meters of the charge, you immediately use the Move Fire Blast on the victim. Roll 1d10 to determine additional effects from the detonation. 1: Cave in – Everyone within 30 meters of the charge in the cavern is automatically hit with the move Rock Tomb. 2: Natural Deterrent – If breaking into a new underground tunnel, it is partially blocked off by the appropriate natural event: underground spring, sand cave in, lava, or Pokémon interaction. 3: Wanton Destruction - Any items, fossils, or Pokémon are destroyed or scared off from the entire cave network. 4-9: Successful demolition 10: Discovery! - You find a buried site, details are left up to your GM.

Spelunking is the Schist.effect: While underground you know if there are other viable exits to the surface or geologically unstable areas. You may also find areas that grant a +3 bonus to your Digging for the Past and Stone Search Feature rolls.

Stone Energizer.effect: The stone gains a second use before being destroyed. You may not use Stone Energizer on the same stone twice.

Stone Polish.effect: After ten hours of work, you may turn that stone into an Evolution Stone. Choose one of the eighteen elemental types. When you touch a Pokémon with an Evolution Stone that matches one of their Elemental Types will forcefully evolve them if they meet the evolution level requirement but has not yet evolved.

The Good Doctor.target: A Pokémon deceased within the last 24 hours.
The Good Doctor.effect: After five hours of work, roll 1d20 and add your SATK modifier. If you roll higher than 20 you create an artificial fossil from the corpse of the Pokémon. This fossil acts as a normal fossil. You may not target Legendary Pokémon with this feature.

Snapshot.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Snapshot.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. Multiply the result by 20. This is the approximate value of the photo. You may try to sell this photograph anywhere.

Action Photo.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Action Photo.trigger: You or an ally are attacked by a Pokémon using a Move Effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier.
Action Photo.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 15, you successfully obtain a photo of the Move. Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. Multiply the total by 30. This is the approximate value of the photo. You may try to sell this photograph anywhere.

Action Photo +.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Action Photo +.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 15, you successfully obtain a photo of the Move. Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. Multiply the total by 45. This is the approximate value of the photo. You may try to sell this photograph anywhere.

Battle Study.effect: While you possess a photograph taken with Action Photo of a specific Move, any use of that Move will require +2 on an Accuracy Check to hit you or your Pokémon.

Camera Flash.effect: Use the Move Flash. You may use this feature as a Free Action when you use Snapshot, Action Photo, or Action Photo+, but must share the same target as that Feature.

Examine.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Examine.trigger: You use Snapshot on a Pokémon or Trainer Effect: You may use the Move Foresight on Snapshot’s Target as a free action.
Examine.effect: You may use the Move Foresight on Snapshot’s Target as a free action. Alternatively, you may, as a Trainer Action, study a Photo you have already taken to use Foresight on the photo’s target.

Natural Shot.effect: When using snapshot on a wild Pokémon, instead of multiplying your Snapshot roll by 20, multiply the result by 45.

Pester Ball A.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Pester Ball A.effect: After 6 seconds of work on your last turn, roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 15 use the move Taunt on the target. Pester Ball A cannot target a Pokémon who has already been successfully hit with any Pester Ball during this encounter.

Pester Ball B.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Pester Ball B.effect: After 6 seconds of work on your last turn, roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 15 use the move Supersonic on the target. Pester Ball B cannot target a Pokémon who has already been successfully hit with any Pester Ball during this encounter.

Pester Ball C.prerequisites: Photographer, a Pester Ball A or B
Pester Ball C.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Pester Ball C.effect: After 12 seconds of work on your last two turns, roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 10 use the move Poison Gas and Sand-Attack on the target. Pester Ball C cannot target a Pokémon who has already been successfully hit with any Pester Ball during this encounter.

Photo Research.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Photo Research.effect: Select a Photo of a Pokémon, Trainer, or Route taken with Snapshot. You gain a single Insight Point of the Photo’s target, as if you had used Did the Homework. You may not use the same Photograph multiple times for Photo Research.

Photo Tutor.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Photo Tutor.effect: Select a Photo taken with Action Photo or Action Photo +, that is of a Move that the target can be learn by Level Up, TM or by Move Tutor. Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 20, the target learns that Move. If you fail, that Photo may not be used again with Photo Tutor. Once you successfully use Photo Tutor on a Pokémon, Photo Tutor may not target that Pokémon ever again.

Tutor Mastery.effect: You may target your own Pokémon up to three times with Photo Tutor. If you later trade away your Pokémon, they forget all but one of the Tutored moves.

Potions Mastery.effect: After one hour of work, create a Potion; or after two hours of work, create a Super Potion; or after four hours of work, create a Hyper Potion. Name the concoction whatever you’d like out of whatever ingredients you’d like.

Repels Mastery.effect: After one hour of work, create a Repel; or after two hours of work, create a Super Repel; or after four hours of work, create a Max Repel; Name the concoction whatever you’d like out of whatever ingredients you’d like.

Body Pills.prerequisites: Scientist, a Pokémon with at least 20 HP stat or Speed stat
Body Pills.effect: After four hours of work, create a HP Up or Carbos. Name the concoction whatever you’d like out of whatever ingredients you’d like.

Defense Pills.prerequisites: Scientist, a Pokémon with at least 20 Defense stat or Special Defense stat
Defense Pills.effect: After four hours of work, create an Iron or Zinc. Name the concoction whatever you’d like out of whatever ingredients you’d like.

Energy Brewer.effect: After four hours of work, create an Ether. like out of whatever ingredients you’d like.

Energy Mastery.effect: After four hours of work, create an Elixir. like out of whatever ingredients you’d like. Name the concoction whatever you’d Name the concoction whatever you’d

Offense Pills.prerequisites: Scientist, a Pokémon with at least 20 Attack stat or Special Attack stat
Offense Pills.effect: After four hours of work, create a Protein or Calcium. Name the concoction whatever you’d like out of whatever ingredients you’d like.

Performance Enhancers.effect: After thirty minutes of work, roll 1d8. On a result of 1, you create a X Attack; on a result of 2, you create a X Defend; on a result of 3, you create a X Special; on a result of 4, you create a X Sp. Def; on a result of 5, you create a X Speed; on a result of 6, you create a X Accuracy; on a result of 7, you create a Dire Hit; on a result of 8, you create a Guard Spec. Name the concoction whatever you’d like out of whatever ingredients you’d like.

Performance Enhancer Mastery.prerequisites: Scientist, Performance Enhancers has been used at least 3 times
Performance Enhancer Mastery.effect: After one hour of work, create either a X Attack, a X Defend, a X Special, a X Sp. Def, a X Speed, a X Accuracy, a Dire Hit or a Guard Spec. This Feature replaces Performance Enhancers. Name the concoction whatever you’d like out of whatever ingredients you’d like.

Frequency Pills.effect: After ten hours of work, create a PP Up. Name the concoction whatever you’d like out of whatever ingredients you’d like.

Status Brewer.effect: After thirty minutes of work, roll 1d6. On a result of 1, you create an Antidote; on a result of 2, you create an Awakening; on a result of 3, you create a Burn Heal; on a result of 4, you create an Ice Heal; on a result of 5, you create a Paralyze Heal; on a result of 6, you create a Full Heal. Name the concoction whatever you’d like out of whatever ingredients you’d like.

Status Mastery.prerequisites: Scientist, Status Brewer has been used at least 3 times
Status Mastery.effect: After one hour of work, create an Antidote, Awakening, Burn Heal, Ice Heal or Paralyze Heal. This Feature replaces Status Brewer. Name the concoction whatever you’d like out of whatever ingredients you’d like.

Trait Suppressant.effect: After one hour of work, create an item with the same effect as a Pomeg, Kelpsy, Qualot, Hondew, Grepa or Tomato Berry. Name the concoction whatever you’d like out of whatever ingredients you’d like.

Family Finder.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Family Finder.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SATK and SDEF modifiers. If the total exceeds 15, you track where this Pokémon lives in the area. If there are other Pokémon of the same species or evolution line in the area, you will either find them there or hints to where they may be. If the total exceeds 20, you locate food sources and other resources Pokémon of this evolutionary line rely upon, which may include berry plants, vegetation, sources of water and other things depending on GM discretion.

Close Watch.frequency: Daily – Every 3 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Close Watch.effect: Multiply your SDEF modifier by 3m. Wild Pokémon will not notice you and you do not need to roll Stealth Checks up to this distance from Wilds.. If you announce your presence, the Pokémon you approach is not instantly provoked but may be startled. You may attempt to befriend the Wild if you don’t provoke it.

Applied Psychology.target: A person that you suspect may be willing to lie.
Applied Psychology.effect: When you declare the use of this feature, specify a statement made by the target and you will immediately know if it is completely truthful. This feature may be used on recorded information. If the target is saying something that they believe is true, then they do not appear to be lying.

Berrydex.effect: You can identify any Berry its properties and what it yields in terms of Contest Stats. When you are making Berries into Poffins, Aprijuice or PokeBlocks, you get +2 to your rolls.

Discriminating Gaze.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Discriminating Gaze.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 10, you identify whether the target Pokémon has an elemental typing, ability, Skill or move not natural to its species. If the total exceeds 20, you know all the specific differences between that Pokémon and a normal member of its species.

Improved Tracking.prerequisites: Watcher, a Pokémon with Tracker Skill
Improved Tracking.effect: Whenever your Pokémon roll to use the Tracker Skill, add 5 to the roll.

Odor Sleuth Tutor.target: A Pokémon who is leveling up.
Odor Sleuth Tutor.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 15, the Pokémon learns the Move, Odor Sleuth. Once you successfully use Odor Sleuth Tutor on a Pokémon, Odor Sleuth Tutor may not target that Pokémon ever again.

Quick Draw.effect: You are a master sketch artist. It takes you less than a minute to sketch anything you see. You can flesh out a sketch over thirty minutes to add more detail. After an hour of work, you can draw anything you’ve seen in the last 24 hours with amazing detail.

Shiny Hunt.frequency: Daily - Every 20 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Shiny Hunt.effect: Roll 1d100. If you rolled under you SDEF modifier multiplied by 3, a shiny Pokémon will appear in the next wild encounter.

Silent Study.effect: If you are using Close Watch, you may approach up to 3 meters away from the wild without alerting the Pokémon unless you want to after approaching with Close Watch. If you do not make it obvious you are there, the Wild will not know you are there.

Type Finder.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Type Finder.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 13, you find hints that indicate what other Pokémon of the same Type live in the area. You must still use other means to find these Pokémon. If the total exceeds 20, you locate food sources and other resources Pokémon of this Type commonly rely upon, which may include berry plants, vegetation, sources of water and other things depending on GM discretion.

Watchful Tactician.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Watchful Tactician.trigger: You start an encounter with wild Pokémon Effect: You may, as a free action, use Game Plan, Game Plan+, or Sabotage at the beginning of this encounter before any other trainers or Pokémon take actions.
Watchful Tactician.effect: You may, as a free action, use Game Plan, Game Plan+, or Sabotage at the beginning of this encounter before any other trainers or Pokémon take actions. You must have the corresponding Feature to use it via Watchful Tactician. Using a Feature this way depletes both a use of that Feature and of Watchful Tactician.

Channel.frequency: At-Will - Drains 10 of user’s HP per use.
Channel.effect: Make a Link Check, then subtract your Defense Stat from your roll. If it is successful, the Pokémon is being Channeled. The Pokémon is considered Helpful. Until this ally is attacked or severely distracted, it will listen to the Mystic’s suggestions and follow the Mystic until they are leaving the Pokémon’s home if it is a wild Pokémon. You have knowledge of the Pokémon’s Move List, Abilities and Skills. You have knowledge of what happened in the Pokémon’s past hour. You and the Channeled Pokémon can communicate telepathically. Any attempt at attacking or capturing the Channeled Pokémon results in the Pokémon fleeing without fail. The Channeled Pokémon will not attack on your command. However, if the Channeled Pokémon is yours, treat it as usual, except it is being “Channeled”. You may only have one Pokémon Channeled at a time. If a Pokémon is more than X meters from you, it is no longer Channeled. X is twice your DEF modifier.

Borrow.effect: Choose a Move on the Channeled Pokémon’s Move List. You may perform this Move, once, as a Trainer Action during the next 24 hours.

Telekinesis.target: Any object that you may lift.
Telekinesis.effect: Use your SATK modifier and multiply it by 10 pounds. This represents the weight you may lift and items you may target with Telekinesis. Use your SATK modifier and multiply that product by 3meters. This is how far the target can be. You may not move that target more than the range of your Telekinesis. You need a line of sight to target the object. You can Shift the target up to X meters/spaces per turn where X is twice your SATK modifier.

Telepathy.target: Any other human, some may be immune to Telepathy
Telepathy.effect: You may read the surface thoughts of other humans, as well as project your surface thoughts to other humans. You may also project telepathic messages to your Pokémon, but may not read your Pokémon’s minds. This leaves Psychic Residue.

Befriend.effect: Give the Pokémon the Helpful title. This ally will remain under the Ranger’s guidance until it is hit by an attack, severely distracted, or has helped the Ranger in a significant manner once, such as by making an attack. You do not get a list of any Skills or moves you cannot deduce yourself, and it cannot be taken from the area you found it in or it will run away. You may only give the Helpful title to one Pokémon at a time.

Companion.effect: Give the Pokémon the Companion title. You may only have one Companion at a time. If you give a new Pokémon the Companion title, you free your previous Companion and replace it with your new one. This process starts the Companion at Loyalty 2 it will be obedient towards the Ranger, regardless of circumstances before being Restrained. The Companion gains experience and levels exactly like a Captured Pokémon, as well as counting as Owned for the purposes of levels and features, but cannot participate in official battles or contests (doing so is fraud). The Pokémon cannot be returned to a Poke Ball, and Pokémon Centers may charge more for healing a Companion. Companions are considered Helpful, but act in combat as if they were owned.

Ancient Pulse.effect: You can identify the abilities of any non-electronic, inorganic Items and know how to activate their abilities and use them.

Channel Friend.effect: Whenever you target a Pokémon with Loyalty 2 or above that you own with Channel, treat your Possession Check as if you rolled 0.

Channel Friend +.effect: Whenever you target a Pokémon with Loyalty 2 or above that you own with Channel , treat your Possession Check as if you rolled 0 and do not lose any HP in order to use Channel. Whenever you target a Loyalty 2 or above Pokémon an ally owns with Channel and their permission, treat your Possession Check as if you rolled 0. You may also channel up to two Pokémon at a time. This Feature replaces Channel Friend.

Channel Mind.effect: All Channeled Pokémon are treated as if their Intelligence Skill is as high as the highest Intelligence Skill among all Channeled Pokémon, or equal to your SATK or ATK modifier. A Pokémon’s Intelligence Skill cannot be higher than 7. A Pokémon’s Intelligence can only be raised up to 6 because of your modifiers.

Experience Shared.effect: You may see through the eyes of any Channeled Pokémon. You may hear anything a Channeled Pokémon can hear. If you are Channeling more than one Pokémon, you may only change between which Pokémon you are sharing senses with once per round of combat.

Mystic Veil.effect: Add your DEF modifier multiplied by 3 to your total HP. This only applies to your human body.

Perfect Vessel.effect: You may Channel up to three Pokémon at the same time.

Prophet.effect: You know lore relating to any local Legendary Pokémon.

Sponge.prerequisites: Mystic, borrowed a move from a loyal Pokémon who has been raised at least 20 levels
Sponge.effect: You may Borrow up to 3 Moves at a time when using Borrow or Possession Borrow. This does not allow you to use Borrow or Possession Borrow multiple times a day - instead you may choose to Borrow multiple moves at once from the same Pokémon during your Daily use of either Feature. The total number of Moves Borrowed a day may not exceed 3. You may not Borrow multiple copies of the same Move in one day.

Tree Hugger.effect: You can speak to plants that are at least 20 years old, and with a limited response they can inform you of anything that may have touched them in the past 24 hours, any Berries that may grow on them, or any Pokémon who live nearby and commonly visit.

Voodoo Shuffle.effect: You may make two Channel attempts as one Trainer Action.

Zoopathy.frequency: At-Will - Drains 10 of user’s HP per use.
Zoopathy.effect: You can talk to the Pokémon in its language for 4 hours.

Extrasensory.frequency: At-Will – Drains 10 of user’s HP per use.

Gravity.frequency: At-Will – Drains 20 of user’s HP per use.

Light Screen.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Light Screen.effect: Use the Move Light Screen. When you do, you can make the Light Screen blocking terrain.

Magic Room.frequency: At-Will – Drains 20 of user’s HP per use.

Probability Control.frequency: At-Will – Drains 15 of user’s HP per use.
Probability Control.effect: You may reroll any die, or have any ally reroll any die. For every time you use this ability per day, past the first use on any day, your GM gets one Murphy’s Law point. They may use this point to have you reroll any die that you haven’t targeted with Probability Control, the GM may only target your dice rolls with Murphy’s Law Points.

Psychic Finder.effect: You can identify if a psychic has affected someone’s mind and identify the meddler, human and Pokémon alike by recognizing Psychic Residue. Also, as a free action, you may also roll 1d20 when using a Feature which leaves Psychic Residue. If your roll plus your SATK modifier is higher than 15, you leave no psychic residue.

Psychic Force.prerequisites: Psychic, 18 SATK, a Pokémon who knows the Move Psychic
Psychic Force.frequency: At-Will - Drains 20 of user’s HP per use.

Psychokinesis.target: Any object that you may lift.
Psychokinesis.effect: You may target objects you know of, but cannot see, with Telekinesis. You do not need a line of sight to move those objects. They must still be within your range of Telekinesis.

Reflect.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Reflect.effect: Use the Move Reflect. When you do, you can make the Reflect blocking terrain.

Authority Figure.prerequisites: Ranger, recognized as having performed distinguished service
Authority Figure.effect: You take on the responsibilities of a ranking Ranger officer. You may give orders to Rangers and police officers. A police chief may overrule your orders to police, but none may overrule your orders to Rangers. When requesting assistance from a local Ranger station on an important issue, you are obligated to receive help in some form except for in extraordinary circumstances.

Befriender.effect: You may subtract an additional 10 from your Restrain rolls, and you may give the Helpful title to two additional Pokémon. You may take this feature one more time, for a total of –20 on Restrain Rolls and five Helpful Pokémon.

Dizzy.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day. Uses 1 Energy Point.
Dizzy.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SPD modifier. If the total is 15 or higher, the Target is Confused. Use of this feature requires your Capture Styler.

Part of The Team.prerequisites: Ranger, Companions that have been with you for a total of two months
Part of The Team.target: Your Companion that has been with you for at least a month.
Part of The Team.effect: Spend one hour talking and negotiating with the target, then the target will agree to being captured by you. After capturing, the Pokémon is still considered your Companion for the purposes of features. You may designate a new Companion as if the previous one had been released without removing the Companion title from the target

Perfect Practice.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day.
Perfect Practice.effect: When Restraining a Pokémon, subtract your ATK or SPD modifier multiplied by 3 from your Restrain roll. This may only be used once per restrain roll. If you successfully restrain a Pokémon when using this feature, you are given a partial list of moves known by the Pokémon, and know of all available Skills.

Poke-Assist.prerequisites: Ranger, one Advanced Class with Ranger as a prerequisite, Level 10.
Poke-Assist.frequency: Daily - Every 15 levels, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Poke-Assist.target: Your Companion or a Helpful Pokémon.
Poke-Assist.effect: For a number of rounds equal to half of either your ATK or SPD modifiers, the target acts on its own in combat, attacking opponents of its own free will without requiring orders. The GM controls Pokémon under the effect of this feature. The target does not flee or retreat if injured while under the effects of this feature. Only one Pokémon may be under the effects of this feature at any given time. This feature may not be active while its user is currently using Dual Wielding.

Resources.effect: You call in support from at least one Ranger in the area. This Ranger will go directly to you and meet you as soon as possible, and assist you with any pressing business, but will not leave the area or otherwise completely abandon their current task unless ordered otherwise by an authority above both of you.

Safe Passage.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Safe Passage.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your ATK and SPD modifiers. If the total exceeds 20, you can make it across the target route without any hostile encounters from any routine wild Pokémon for the day.

Specialist Befriender.prerequisites: Ranger, used Befriend on a Pokémon with the Skill you chose for this Feature.
Specialist Befriender.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature
Specialist Befriender.target: Any Route, Town, City, Mountain, etc.
Specialist Befriender.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your ATK or SPD modifier, if the total is 15 or higher a Pokémon with your chosen Skill appears nearby. Any attempt at attacking or capturing the Pokémon results in the Pokémon fleeing without fail. The Pokémon must still be made Helpful or Companion to use. You must take this Feature again for a different Skill.

Styler Master.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day. Uses 1 Energy Point.
Styler Master.effect: Make a Restrain Check. You may target the owned Pokémon with Befriend. Owned Pokémon will not attack their own trainer unless they are Loyalty 1 or lower. Pokémon of Loyalty 2 or higher will not attack allies of their trainer. Owned Pokémon are Helpful until having been returned by their trainer, or until they have attacked a valid target. Attempting to force a high loyalty Pokémon to attack their allies will negate the restrain. You do not learn the Pokémon’s Loyalty value upon restraining.

Ward.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Ward.trigger: You successfully restrain a wild Pokémon.
Ward.effect: The wild Pokémon flees without fail. Roll 1d20 and add your ATK and SPD modifiers, if the total is higher than 20, the restrained Pokémon and any wild Pokémon of a lower level than the target who are its allies flee without fail.

Team Spirit.effect: Each of your Pokémon are treated as if one of their Combat Stages are raised 1 Combat Stage. The Combat Stage raised depends on what the highest Base Stat of your other active Pokémon is, ignoring HP. For example, if one of your active Pokémon’s highest stat, ignoring HP, is Attack, the other active Pokémon’s Attack will be raised 1 Combat Stage. When switching, the Combat Stages are lowered and then raised again depending on the stats of each active Pokémon.

Together!.effect: The targets may combine two moves that share the same Stat, Elemental Type, and can each legally target the target according to each move’s range. When you use a Feature that combines two Moves, the Combined Move happens on the slower participant’s turn; the faster participant shifts and uses the move along with the slower on that turn. If the moves are of the same type, the Combined Move shares that type. If combined moves are of differing types, the Combined Move ignores weakness, resistance or immunity. The Moves must both be able to legally target the target according to each Move’s Range. Neither Move can have the Scatter keyword. The Move’s Accuracy Check is the average of the two Moves’ Accuracy Checks. The Damage Dice Roll is both of the Moves’ Damage Dice Rolls combined. Treat the Frequencies of the Moves chosen as you would if you weren’t combining Moves. All Effects are added to the new, combined Move. Combined Moves may not be used by only one Pokémon.

Brace Each Other!.effect: As long as your two active Pokémon are adjacent, neither can suffer Push damage nor be Pushed. If your Pokémon are adjacent to you, you cannot be Pushed nor suffer Push damage either.

Combine Them!.effect: The targets may combine two moves that share the same Stat, and can each legally target the target according to each move’s range. This feature does not replace Together!

Dual Assault.frequency: Daily - every 8 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.
Dual Assault.trigger: Your Pokémon damages an opponent your other active Pokémon has damaged this turn.
Dual Assault.effect: The damaged target loses one Combat Stage for one of your active Pokémon's highest stat.

Dual Interference.target: A Pokémon that has been hit by two of your different Pokémon.
Dual Interference.effect: The targeted foe must roll +2 on Accuracy checks during its next turn when targeting your Pokémon.

Taking One for A Friend.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Taking One for A Friend.trigger: One of your two adjacent active Pokémon is targeted by a Move.
Taking One for A Friend.effect: Roll 1d20 and add you ATK modifier. If you roll higher than 15, the active Pokémon who was not targeted may intercept the Move for the targeted Pokémon. If the Move’s area of effect still hits both Pokémon, the Pokémon who is intercepting the hit may take both sets of damage before applying Defense or Special Defense.

Taking One for A Friend +.prerequisites: Tag Battler, Taking One for A Friend
Taking One for A Friend +.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Taking One for A Friend +.trigger: One of your adjacent active Pokémon or ally’s Pokémon is targeted by a Move.
Taking One for A Friend +.effect: Roll 1d20 and add you ATK modifier. If you roll higher than 10, the active Pokémon who was not targeted may intercept the Move for the targeted Pokémon. If the move’s area of effect still hits both Pokémon, the Pokémon who is intercepting the hit may take both sets of damage before applying Defense or Special Defense. This Feature replaces Taking One for A Friend.

With Them!.target: Your one active Pokémon while you are in a trainer battle with allies.
With Them!.effect: Your Pokémon and one of your ally’s Pokémon may combine two moves that share the same Stat, Elemental Type, and can each legally target the target according to each move’s range.

Try This!.effect: The targets may combine two No Damage moves, that do not directly affect HP, have the Wall keyword or copy other moves and can each legally target the target according to each move’s range.

Synchronized Shove.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.
Synchronized Shove.trigger: Your Pokémon damages a target one of your other Pokémon has damaged this round.
Synchronized Shove.effect: The enemy is pushed a number of meters in a direction of your choice equal to the sum of your active Pokémon’s Power Skills.

Teamwork.effect: Each of your Pokémon are treated as if one of their Speed Skills are raised by an amount equal to half of your ATK modifier. The Skill raised depends on what the highest Speed Skill of your other active Pokémon is.

Everstone Finder.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SATK modifier. If the result is above 15, you find an Everstone.

Hidden Strength.effect: When you or any of your Prime Unown uses one of its Hidden Powers, you may choose to, instead of it acting as a Burst, have it act as a Column 1-meter wide, 15 meters long. Whenever your Prime Unown use Hidden Power, they always add +1 STAB to their damage roll, regardless of their typing.

Underdog Feature.frequency: Daily - Every 6 Levels gained, you may perform this feature another time per day.
Underdog Feature.target: Your not-fully-evolved Pokémon with a different Typing than its evolved forms.
Underdog Feature.effect: Your ATK modifier is X. For X rounds, the Target replaces its Types with that of one of its evolved forms.

Capable.effect: All of the Target’s existing numerical Skills are increased by 1. You may only apply this Feature to one Target at a time. Changing what is benefitting from Capable takes a trainer action.

Champ in The Making.prerequisites: Underdog, has prevented the evolution of a Pokémon whose final stage evolves at a minimum of Level 40
Champ in The Making.effect: Choose one Stat. For one battle, the Target increases the chosen Stat by an amount equal to its final stage’s chosen Base Stat.

Everstone Improvement.effect: After twenty minutes of work, roll 1d20 and add your SATK modifier. If the result is above 20, designate a Type. From then on, the Target, when held by a Pokémon of the designated Type that is not-fully-evolved or is a first-stage Pokémon that cannot evolve, allows that Pokémon to deal +1 STAB.

Everstone Perfection.effect: After twenty minutes of work, roll 1d20 and add your SATK modifier. If the result is above 20, create an Eviolite that additionally has the Everstone’s abilities.

Forceful Technique.effect: Your not-fully-evolved Pokémon may add STAB to Moves as if they were 10 Levels higher. There is no STAB greater than the Level 91-100 group.

Hidden Strength +.frequency: Daily - Every 10 Levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Hidden Strength +.target: Your Pokémon, which has at least one evolutionary stage remaining.
Hidden Strength +.effect: Choose a Move that one of the Target’s evolved forms can learn naturally. The Target may use that move once.

Hidden Strength X.frequency: Daily - Every 8 Levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Hidden Strength X.target: Your Pokémon, which has at least one evolutionary stage remaining.
Hidden Strength X.effect: Choose a Tutor Move or Level Up Move that one of the Target’s evolved forms can learn. The Target may use that move once. This may only apply to moves learned at X levels above the target’s level or lower where X is your ATK or SATK modifier doubled. This Feature replaces Hidden Strength+.

Highly Capable.frequency: Daily - Every 5 Levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Highly Capable.effect: Your SATK modifier is X. For X minutes, all of the Target’s existing numerical Skills are increased by 2.

Incredibly Capable.effect: Your ATK modifier halved is X. For X minutes, the Target gains your choice of one of the Skills of one of its evolved forms.

The Bigger They Are.effect: For every evolutionary stage, the Target is below the Pokémon receiving its damage, add your ATK and SATK modifier to the damage before applying Defense or Special Defense.

Versatile Technique.effect: Your not-fully-evolved Pokémon may add STAB to Moves as if they were all Types that any of its evolved forms have.

Early Evolution.effect: Your DEF modifier is X. Your Pokémon who level up by level requirement may evolve up to X levels earlier if they meet their requirements.

Mega Stone Maker.target: One of your fully evolved Pokémon with at least 2 Loyalty
Mega Stone Maker.effect: After five hours of focus, create a Mega Stone exclusively for use the targeted Pokémon. When creating your Mega Stone, decide which Base Stats are boosted while the Pokémon is using the created Mega Stone. You can modify the Base Stats by a total of 10, but no individual stat can be modified by more than 4. Do not modify the HP base stat and do not affect any Base Stats negatively. Discuss with your GM how the Mega Stone affects you Pokémon’s Type and Ability.

Devolve!.effect: You may use this Feature when you successfully damage a Pokémon. Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the result is greater than 22 and the target has less than 10% of its HP left, it is returned to a previous stage of its evolutionary line. Adjust its stats appropriately, but its Move set is unaltered. That Pokémon may not evolve for another 5 levels unless that Pokémon is already above level 50.

Early Evolution +.effect: Your DEF modifier doubled is X. Your Pokémon who level up by level requirement may evolve up to X levels earlier if they meet their requirements. This Feature replaces Early Evolution.

Elemental Evolution.prerequisites: Evolver, 16 DEF, evolved a Pokémon with an Elemental Stone
Elemental Evolution.target: An allied Pokémon or allied Trainer’s Pokémon, which can evolve through
Elemental Evolution.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your DEF modifier. If the result exceeds 18, the Pokémon spontaneously evolves. If you fail the check, the Pokémon loses 2 levels, and its experience is lowered appropriately for it to be 2 levels under where it just was. Lower stats appropriately and remove Moves appropriately.

Keystone Mod: Burst!!!.prerequisites: Evolver, Keystone Mod: Charge, Overload, and Time
Keystone Mod: Burst!!!.target: Two of your Pokémon with Mega Stones
Keystone Mod: Burst!!!.effect: Mega Evolve two of your Pokémon at the same time. You may not use this feature if you’ve already Mega Evolved a Pokémon today and you may not Mega Evolve any more Pokémon today.

Keystone Mod: Charge.effect: You may activate your Key Stone twice, Daily. You may not target the same Pokémon with your Key Stone on the same day. Your Key Stone can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon at a time.

Keystone Mod: Overload.effect: Your Mega Evolved Pokémon deal +2 STAB when using Moves that apply STAB, losing 1d20 subtracted by your DEF modifier HP each time they do.

Keystone Mod: Time.effect: Your Mega Evolutions last for up to half an hour or until the Mega Evolved Pokémon is brought down to 0HP.

Mega Scouter.effect: You can identify whether any Pokémon has a Mega Stone that it can use to Mega Evolve by looking at it.

Potential Unleashed.effect: If you own a Mega Stone that corresponds to a Mega Evolution that appears in the Pokédex, your not-fully-evolved Pokémon that will eventually be fully compatible with the Mega Stone may already use the Mega Stone. For example, if you own Blazikenite, your Torchic or Combusken could use it to turn into Mega Blaziken.

Natural Arrangement.effect: When any of your Pokémon learn a Tutor or TM Move, you may place it on your Pokémon’s Natural Move list. When doing so, you may only place up to two Moves that would normally be on your Pokémon’s Tutor/TM Move list onto its Natural Move list.

Tutor Pushing.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Tutor Pushing.effect: After fifteen minutes of working with the target, roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 15, chose a Move from the Tutor Move list that the target Pokémon can learn. Teach the target that Tutor Move as if you had used a TM. Once you successfully use Tutor Pushing on a Pokémon, Tutor Pushing may not target that Pokémon ever again. Pokémon owned by Move Tutors gain one “Tutor Point” every 5 levels. When a trainer acquires a Pokémon, it retroactively gains all unspent Tutor Points for all its previously earned levels. To use certain Move Tutor Features, the targeted Pokémon must have a certain amount Tutor Points. Upon the successful use of certain Move Tutor

Capability Tutoring.effect: After fifteen minutes of working with the target, choose a Move on the targeted Pokémon’s Level-up, Egg, TM or Tutor list that grants or modifies a Skill that is not Aura. The Pokémon gains that Skill. Capability Pushing may not target that Pokémon ever again.

Elemental Unlock.effect: Choose an Elemental Type. After fifteen minutes of working with the target, the target Pokémon can now learn TMs of that Elemental Type, regardless of its other types. Elemental Unlock may not target that Pokémon ever again. You may take Elemental Unlock up to 3 times.

HM Smith.effect: After four hours of work, change a TM in to an HM.

Physical Tutoring.prerequisites: Move Tutor, a Pokémon with an Attack stat of 30 or higher
Physical Tutoring.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Physical Tutoring.effect: After fifteen minutes of working with the target, roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 15, chose a TM that teaches a Move that uses the Attack Stat as long as the target Pokémon can learn that TM. Teach the target that TM as if you had used a TM. Once you successfully use Physical Pushing on a Pokémon, Physical Pushing may not target that Pokémon ever again.

Special Tutoring.prerequisites: Move Tutor, a Pokémon with a Special Attack stat of 30 or higher
Special Tutoring.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Special Tutoring.effect: After fifteen minutes of working with the target, roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 15, chose a TM that teaches a Move that uses the Special Attack Stat as long as the target Pokémon can learn that TM. Teach the target that TM as if you had used a TM. Once you successfully use Special Pushing on a Pokémon, Special Pushing may not target that Pokémon ever again.

Status Tutoring.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Status Tutoring.effect: After fifteen minutes of working with the target, roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 15, choose a TM that teaches a Move that does not apply the Attack or Special Attack Stat that the target Pokémon can learn. Teach the target that TM as if you had used a TM. Once you successfully use Status Pushing on a Pokémon, Status Pushing may not target that Pokémon ever again.

TM King.prerequisites: Move Tutor, TM Smith, TM Pushing
TM King.effect: Remove the following line from TM Maker’s  “You may not create the same TM more than once with this Feature.” Remove the following line from TM Smith’s Effect: “You may not create a TM with the same Move more than once with this Feature.” Remove the following line from TM Pushing’s Effect: “You may never select that TM again with TM pushing.”

TM Maker.prerequisites: Move Tutor, 17 SDEF, successfully used Move Tutor Features at least 3 times
TM Maker.effect: After four hours of work, roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 20, chose a TM you’ve Pushed onto a Pokémon. You create that TM. You may not create the same TM more than once with this Feature.

TM Pushing.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
TM Pushing.effect: After fifteen minutes of working with the target, roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 15, chose a TM from the TM list that the target Pokémon can learn. Teach the target that TM as if you had used a TM. You may never select that TM again with TM pushing.

TM Smith.effect: After four hours of work, roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 20, chose a Move that isn’t on the TM list that one of your Pokémon can perform. You create a TM with that Move. Give it any number above 101, or add

Tutor Capacity.prerequisites: Move Tutor, Used Tutor Pushing on 5 Pokémon
Tutor Capacity.effect: Remove the following line from Tutor Pushing’s  “Once you successfully use Tutor Pushing on a Pokémon, Tutor Pushing may not target that Pokémon ever again.”

Pok\u00e9dex Modifications.effect: You may alter the Pokédex to have a Radar that works at a great range. You may alter that value from anywhere between a 5m radius to a 15m radius. The Pokédex can also identify how many wilds the radar detects and which direction they are. The radar cannot detect Pokémon who are not actively moving.

Mech Arm.effect: You now have a mechanical arm you can wear over either your right or left arm. Add 2 points to your ATK stat. The Mech Arm has extending poles that can support the Arm’s weight when you are getting tired. The arm weighs 40 pounds.

Bullet Punch.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.

Drill Run.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.

Hacker.effect: You can access any computer or network without rolling a Skill Check. Any further Programming skill checks are made with a +5 to your rolls.

Learning Curve.effect: You may add X to your SATK or SDEF modifier for the next five minutes. X is the total of different Pokémon owned in your Pokédex divided by 10.

Pokeball Traps.effect: At any given time, you may have X of your Pokeballs marked to be usable by Pokeball traps. X is your SPD modifier. Applying or removing a modification to make a Pokeball usable with Pokeball Traps takes 10 minutes. Set or throw your Pokeball down anywhere. At any time you may, as a Trainer Action, trigger a Pokeball set by Pokeball Traps to either capture an adjacent Pokémon if it is empty or to release your Pokémon from a filled Pokeball.

Poke Ball Arm Cannon.effect: Your Mech Arm can now fire Poke Balls, to send out your own or capture wilds. When firing any Poke Ball at a wild, subtract 12 from the Poke Ball’s capture rate. Also the Poke Ball deals 1d8 damage to the wild that cannot be reduced by Defense or Special Defense.

Poke Bots.effect: After ten hours of work, lose 40 HP, then finish making a small remote-controlled Pokémon robot that is .5m tall, of any species or general appearance of your choice. It can move 5m per turn and can hold any small object. It can be remotely controlled up to 25m away. Pokémon with the Wired Skill that you own do not need to make a roll to possess one of your Poke Bots. When using the Poke Bots Feature, you can modify a Poke Bot you’ve already made instead of making a new one up to three times. Basic Poke Bots have 20 HP and take Super-Effective damage from Fire, Electric, Ground and Rock Moves, unless possessed by a Wired Pokémon, in which case the Poke Bot’s Type is the same as the possessing Pokémon. -Additional Features you can add to your Poke Bot upon further uses of the Poke Bots Feature: Camera, Video Camera (requires Camera Additional Feature), 20 additional HP, can use the Move Ember Daily, can use the move Thundershock Daily, increased remote control radius to 200m, can fly.

Raft Arm.prerequisites: Engineer, Mech Arm, twelve hours to modify your Mech Arm
Raft Arm.effect: Your Mech Arm is now buoyant and has a compartment that reveals a propeller. In water, your Surface Speed is 8 or 6 Underwater.

Scanner.effect: You can see a percentage when you look at a Pokémon that represents how much of its total HP it has remaining.

Weapon Arm.prerequisites: Engineer, Mech Arm, 12 hours to modify your Mech Arm
Weapon Arm.effect: You may attach a Melee Weapon Attachment to your Mech Arm. You may attach Artificer-Crafted Weapon Attachments. You always have the “Weapon of Choice” Feature for Arms Attacks made using your Mech Arm, and you may add your SPD modifier to damage rolls instead of ATK, or SATK. You cannot be disarmed of your Weapon Attachment, and it may be crafted as being retractable into your Mech Arm.

Throwing Master.effect: You gain the feat “Weapon of Choice” for all Short-Ranged weapons of your choice. You may throw Short-Ranged Weapons and Pokeballs an additional X meters, where X is your SPD modifier.

Quick Switch.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.
Quick Switch.trigger: Your Pokémon Faints; or an opponent sends out a Pokémon
Quick Switch.effect: You may return and send out a Pokémon as a Free Action. You may perform this Feature on your turn without a Trigger.

Bounce Juggle.effect: After successfully hitting a second target with Bounce Shot, you may return the thrown item to your hand.

Bounce Shot.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.
Bounce Shot.trigger: A Thrown item hits its intended target or area.
Bounce Shot.effect: The thrown item is bounced X meters in any direction. X to your SPD modifier. This can cause your Features to hit targets that are otherwise out of range or to hit a second target. Roll for accuracy when attempting to hit a second target.

Emergency Release.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.
Emergency Release.effect: You may send out a Pokémon as an Interrupt.

Enter Like Lightning.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Enter Like Lightning.trigger: You release a Pokémon from its Pokeball
Enter Like Lightning.effect: Use the move Flash, Burst from where you released your Pokémon. For this round, add your SPD modifier to your Pokémon’s Speed Stat for the purposes of determining initiative.

Fast Draw.effect: Once per encounter, you may use an Item on yourself or an allied Trainer or Pokémon as a Free Action.

First Blood.trigger: You release a Pokémon from its pokeball
First Blood.effect: Your Pokémon may use a Move with the Dash keyword as an interrupt as soon as it is sent out. Moves used in this way have the additional effect of pushing the target 5 meters. This consumes your Command action for the round.

Fling.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.
Fling.effect: Use the move Fling. Any item in your possession can count as a Held Item when you use this Move.

No Hands!.effect: You may hide up to 3 Pokeball-sized or smaller items on your person which are undetectable by search. You may activate pokeballs without the use of your hands.

Pokeball Cascade.effect: Pokémon you own add half of your SPD mod to their speeds for the purpose of calculating initiative on the turn they are sent out.

Quick Switch +.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.
Quick Switch +.trigger: Your Pokémon Faints; or an opponent sends out a Pokémon
Quick Switch +.effect: You may return and send out a Pokémon as a Free Action. You may perform this Feature on your turn without a Trigger.

Round Trip.effect: You may immediately switch your Pokémon that just performed a move for another of your Pokémon. You may not issue commands to this Pokémon.

Tag In.effect: The Pokémon you are recalling immediately uses the Move Baton Pass.

Advanced Design.effect: After fifteen minutes, lose 10 HP and then create a Dusk, Dive, Heal, Luxury, Net, Nest, Quick, Repeat, or Timer Ball.

Conditional Advantage.effect: If you would subtract an additional amount due to special circumstances defined by the Pokeball’s type - such as when throwing a Dusk Ball at night - you may subtract your SPD modifier from the capture roll. Mechanics: All Pokeballs have, by default, a single Seal Slot on which seals can be applied. Using their Jail Break feature, Pokeball Designers may add an additional two slots for a total of three.

Apricorn Shift.effect: After working for fifteen minutes, combine the targeted Apricorns into a single Apricorn of your choice.

Apricorn Smith.effect: After working for fifteen minutes, lose 10 HP, then roll 1d20 and add your SPD modifier. It the total exceeds 12, you change the target Apricorn into its corresponding Pokeball. If the total is 11 or below, you ruin the targeted Apricorn.

Ball Modification: Habitat.effect: After working for fifteen minutes, lose 10 HP, then add one of the following keywords to the targeted Dive Ball: Arctic, Mountain, Desert, Grassland, Rainforest, Forest, Tundra, Taiga, Marsh, Beach, Freshwater, Urban or Cave. Instead of when underwater or underground, the modified Poke Ball subtracts 15 from its Capture Rolls when thrown in an area that corresponds with the keyword chosen.

Ball Modification: Power.effect: After working for fifteen minutes, lose 10 HP, then you change the Nest Ball into a Power Ball. It retains the conditional capture modifier of a Nest Ball, but additionally, when a Power Ball captures a target, roll 1d20 and add your SPD modifier. On a result of 15 or better, the captured Pokémon is raised 1d4 levels upon capture. On a result of 25 or better, the target is raised 2d4 levels upon capture instead.

Ball Modification: Type.effect: After working for fifteen minutes, lose 10 HP, then you change the Net Ball into an Earth Ball, Haunt Ball, Solid Ball, Heat Ball, Mystic Ball, Air Ball, Mold Ball, or Fine Ball. The properties of these unique balls are identical to the Net Ball, but instead of affecting Bug and Water types like the Net Ball, these Balls correspond to the following types. Earth Ball; Grass and Ground; Haunt Ball, Dark and Ghost; Solid Ball, Rock and Steel; Heat Ball, Electric and Fire; Mystic Ball, Dragon and Psychic; Air Ball, Flying and Ice; Mold Ball, Poison and Fighting; Fine Ball, Normal and Fairy.

Booster Seal.effect: After working for fifteen minutes, lose 10 HP, then choose any numbered Skill. You create a Booster Seal that corresponds to the chosen Skill. Pokémon kept within a pokeball with a Booster Seal gain a +2 bonus to the chosen Skill if you chose a Movement Skill, or +1 if you chose Intelligence, Jump, or Power. Up to three Booster Seals may be applied to a pokeball, but each must raise a different Skill.

Combat Seal.effect: After working for fifteen minutes, lose 10 HP, then choose a Pokémon stat other than HP. You create a Stat Seal of the chosen stat. Whenever a Pokémon with full HP is released from a pokeball with a Stat Seal, the corresponding stat is raised by 1 Combat Stage. Only one Stat Seal may be applied to a pokeball.

Contest Seal.effect: After working for fifteen minutes, lose 10 HP, then you create a Contest Seal. Pokémon captured in a ball with a Contest Seal get a +1d2 bonus during the Introduction Stage of a contest. It costs 500 to use Contest Seal. Up to three Contest Seals may be applied to a pokeball.

Jail Break.effect: After working for fifteen minutes, lose 10 HP, then create an additional Seal slot on the target pokeball, or you may remove a Seal thereby opening a Seal slot. Seals removed in this way are broken and cannot be repaired. You may use this feature to create up to 2 additional Seal slots, up to a maximum of 3 slots.

Save Ball.effect: After working for three hours, lose 25 HP, then combine a White Apricorn, Red Apricorn, Blue Apricorn, Black Apricorn, Pink Apricorn, Green Apricron and a Yellow Apricorn to make a Save Ball. A Save Ball can capture a Pokémon who was felled on the previous turn of an encounter. It has no Capture Rate bonuses or penalties.

Beautiful Advantage.effect: X is your SDEF modifier. Add X to your Pokémon’s appearance appeal in the first round of a Beauty Contest. Your Pokémon are Beautiful. All of your Pokémon with Loyalty 2 or higher may be targeted as if they have a Beauty Ribbon, but do not actually have one. Your Pokémon may have up to 20 points in their Beauty Contest Stat, and burn up to 5 points of its Beauty Stat on a single move.

Beautiful Offense.target: Your Pokémon with a Beauty Contest Ribbon
Beautiful Offense.effect: Mark your Pokémon with Beauty Ribbons as “Superior.” When adding stats during level up for Superior Pokémon, while the Pokémon is still yours, you may add to the Pokémon’s Special Attack Stat and ignore Base Relation.

Beautiful Grace.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Beautiful Grace.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Beauty Ribbon.
Beautiful Grace.effect: The target gains the Serene Grace Pokémon ability, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has Serene Grace, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled.

Beautiful Pull.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Beautiful Pull.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Beauty Ribbon.
Beautiful Pull.effect: The target gains the Shadow Tag Pokémon ability, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has Shadow Tag, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled.

Beautiful Shine.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Beautiful Shine.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Beauty Ribbon.
Beautiful Shine.effect: The target gains the Magic Guard Pokémon ability, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has Magic Guard, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled.

Beautiful Skin.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Beautiful Skin.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Beauty Ribbon.
Beautiful Skin.effect: The target gains the Marvel Scale Pokémon ability, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has Marvel Scale, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled

Beauty\u2019s Allies.prerequisites: Beauty Modeler, 1 Smart Ribbon or 1 Tough Ribbon
Beauty\u2019s Allies.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Beauty\u2019s Allies.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Beauty Ribbon.
Beauty\u2019s Allies.effect: When the target uses a Smart Move or Tough Move in a Contest, you may choose to have the Move not lower the Voltage. Battle Effect: Once per combat, if the target is attacked, the attacker must role +X to hit where X is your SDEF modifier.

Dangerous Allure.effect: Your Pokémon with at least one Beauty Ribbon have the Alluring Skill. When you, your Pokémon, an ally or ally’s Pokémon, target any wild Pokémon who has attacked one of your Pokémon affected by Dangerous Allure within the same round, or last round, of combat, the Accuracy Check is -2.

Fabulous Max.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels, you may use this feature another time per day.
Fabulous Max.target: Your Pokémon with at least 15 in their Beauty Stat.
Fabulous Max.effect: When the target uses a Move that has a Beauty Contest Type, the move gains the keyword Burst with a size of 2 if Melee, or Blast with a size of 2 if ranged. If the move is already a Burst or Blast, increase the size of the Move’s Range by 2. This Feature may only be applied once per move.

Mind Makeover.effect: Roll 1d20 and your SDEF modifier. If your result is 16 or greater, you may change the target’s Nature to Bashful, Modest, Mild, Rash or Quiet.

Cute Advantage.effect: X is your SDEF modifier. Add X to your Pokémon’s appearance appeal in the first round of a Cute Contest. Your Pokémon are Cute. All of your Pokémon with Loyalty 2 or higher may be targeted as if they have a Cute Ribbon, but do not actually have one. Your Pokémon may have up to 20 points in their Cute Contest Stat, and burn up to 5 points of its Cute Stat on a single move.

Cute Moves.target: Your Pokémon with a Cute Contest Ribbon
Cute Moves.effect: Mark your Pokémon with Cute Ribbons as “Speedy.” When adding stats during level up for Speedy Pokémon, while the Pokémon is still yours, you may add to the Pokémon’s Speed Stat and ignore Base Relation.

Cute Chance.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Cute Chance.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Cute Ribbon.
Cute Chance.effect: The target gains the Super Luck Pokémon ability, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has Super Luck, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled.

Cute Charm.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Cute Charm.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Cute Ribbon.
Cute Charm.effect: The target gains the Cute Charm Pokémon ability, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has Cute Charm, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled.

Cute Feet.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Cute Feet.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Cute Ribbon.
Cute Feet.effect: The target gains the Quick Feet Pokémon ability, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has Quick Feet, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled.

Cute Find.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Cute Find.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Cute Ribbon.
Cute Find.effect: The target gains the Pick Up Pokémon ability, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has Pick Up, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled.

Diversion Tactics.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.
Diversion Tactics.target: Your Pokémon with a Cute Ribbon
Diversion Tactics.effect: As long as your Pokémon has not yet performed a Move this round during combat, they immediately perform one of the following Moves: Captivate, Charm, Fake Tears or Tickle.

Infantilization.target: Your Pokémon or an ally’s Pokémon.
Infantilization.effect: Roll 1d20 and your SDEF modifier. If your result is 16 or greater, you may change the target’s Nature to Serious, Timid, Hasty, Jolly or Naive.

Too Cute to Hurt.trigger: Your Pokémon with a Cute Ribbons is targeted by an attack.
Too Cute to Hurt.effect: Roll 1d20 and your SDEF modifier. If the result is 16 or greater, your Pokémon convinces the attacker to target a different Pokémon. The attacker keeps their accuracy roll, and must use the same move, but it can choose its new target. If there are no other available targets, the feature fails.

Smart Advantage.effect: X is your SDEF modifier. Add X to your Pokémon’s appearance appeal in the first round of a Smart Contest. Your Pokémon are Smart. All of your Pokémon with Loyalty 2 or higher may be targeted as if they have a Smart Ribbon, but do not actually have one. Your Pokémon may have up to 20 points in their Smart Contest Stat, and burn up to 5 points of its Smart Stat on a single move.

Smart Defense.target: Your Pokémon with a Smart Contest Ribbon
Smart Defense.effect: Mark your Pokémon with Smart Ribbons as “Aware.” When adding stats during level up for Aware Pokémon, while the Pokémon is still yours, you may add to the Pokémon’s Special Defense Stat and ignore Base Relation.

Intelligent Design.target: Your Pokémon or an ally’s Pokémon.
Intelligent Design.effect: Roll 1d20 and your SDEF modifier. If your result is 16 or greater, you may change the target’s Nature to Calm, Gentle, Sassy, Sickly or Careful.

Linguistics Lesson.target: Your Pokémon with high loyalty, Intelligence 6
Linguistics Lesson.effect: After 20 hours of work, the target can now speak human languages. Upon activation of Linguistics Lesson, the target will be able to have remedial conversations, but within one week should be able to hold discussion as complicated as your conversations. Linguistics Lesson may target the same Pokémon more than once, teaching a new human language each time.

Schooling.target: Your Pokémon with a Smart Ribbon
Schooling.effect: After 10 hours of work, raise the target’s Intelligence Skill 1 level permanently. Schooling may not be used on a Pokémon with an Intelligence Skill level of 6 or higher.

Smart Details.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Smart Details.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Smart Ribbon.
Smart Details.effect: The target gains the Anticipation Pokémon ability, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has Anticipation, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled.

Smart Focus.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Smart Focus.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Smart Ribbon.
Smart Focus.effect: The target gains the Download Pokémon ability, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has Download, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled.

Smart Planning.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Smart Planning.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Smart Ribbon.
Smart Planning.effect: The target gains the Adaptability Pokémon ability, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has Adaptability, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled.

Smart Skin.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Smart Skin.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Smart Ribbon.
Smart Skin.effect: The target gains the Filter Pokémon ability, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has Filter, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled.

Tough Advantage.effect: X is your SPD modifier. Add X to your Pokémon’s appearance appeal in the first round of a Tough Contest. Your Pokémon are Tough. All of your Pokémon with Loyalty 2 or higher may be targeted as if they have a Tough Ribbon, but do not actually have one. Your Pokémon may have up to 20 points in their Tough Contest Stat, and burn up to 5 points of its Tough Stat on a single move.

Tough Defense.target: Your Pokémon with a Tough Contest Ribbon
Tough Defense.effect: Mark your Pokémon with Tough Ribbons as “Tank.” When adding stats during level up for Tank Pokémon, while the Pokémon is still yours, you may add to the Pokémon’s Defense Stat and ignore Base Relation.

Burly Start.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.
Burly Start.target: A Pokémon with a Tough Stat of 5 or higher.
Burly Start.effect: The Pokémon gains Temporary HP equal to twice their Tough Stat.

Head Strong.target: Your Pokémon or an ally’s Pokémon.
Head Strong.effect: Roll 1d20 and your SPD modifier. If your result is 16 or greater, you may change the target’s Nature to Stark, Bold, Impish, Lax or Relaxed.

Muscle Up.target: Your Pokémon with a Tough Contest Ribbon
Muscle Up.effect: After ten hours of work, raise the target’s Power Skill 1 level permanently. Muscle Up may not be used on a Pokémon with a Power Skill level of 7 or higher.

Tough Blood.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Tough Blood.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Tough Ribbon.
Tough Blood.effect: The target gains the Big Pecks Pokémon ability, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has Big Pecks, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled.

Tough Hide.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Tough Hide.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Tough Ribbon.
Tough Hide.effect: The target gains the Rough Skin Pokémon ability, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has Rough Skin, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled.

Tough Spirit.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Tough Spirit.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Tough Ribbon.
Tough Spirit.effect: The target gains the Reckless Pokémon ability, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has Reckless, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled.

Tough Protectors.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Tough Protectors.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Tough Ribbon.
Tough Protectors.effect: The target gains the Battle Armor Pokémon ability, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has Battle Armor, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled.

Aura Reading.effect: You can read the Aura of your target. Everything has an Aura, but not many can access their Aura. Auras can be any color.
