The shade of the color varies from very tinted to very darkened. If the color of the Aura is tinted, the target has little negative intentions. If the color of the Aura is very blackened, the target has many malicious intentions. An Aura’s shade can be anywhere in between. You may also project your intent to Trainers or Pokémon. This does not allow you to read their minds.

Vacuum Wave.frequency: Daily – Every 6 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Vacuum Wave.effect: Use the Move Vacuum Wave. Use your Attack instead of Special Attack for damage.

Aura Sphere.prerequisites: Aura User, seen the move Aura Sphere performed
Aura Sphere.frequency: Daily – Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Aura Sphere.effect: Use the Move Aura Sphere. Use your Attack instead of Special Attack for damage.

Aura Thoughts.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Aura Thoughts.effect: After ten minutes of focus, you know the general course of actions the target intends to take such as, ‘Meet with associate’ ‘Fight our enemy’ ‘Destroy this location’ ‘Impress close friend’. These intentions can be misinterpreted by the Aura User, like reading the intention to harm someone, when it is simply a friendly competition. These intentions cannot be hidden by Voltorb Flip or the Mind Lock Skill.

Dark Pulse.prerequisites: Aura User, has suffered a Dark Pulse attack
Dark Pulse.frequency: Daily – Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Dark Pulse.effect: Use the Move Dark Pulse. Use your Attack instead of Special Attack for damage.

Drain Punch.prerequisites: Aura User, a Pokémon who knows Drain Punch

Dragon Pulse.prerequisites: Aura User, has suffered a Dragon Pulse attack
Dragon Pulse.frequency: Daily – Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Dragon Pulse.effect: Use the Move Dragon Pulse. Use your Attack instead of Special Attack for damage.

Fire Punch.prerequisites: Aura User, has suffered a Fire Punch attack
Fire Punch.frequency: Daily – Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.

Focus Blast.prerequisites: Aura User, Vacuum Wave, has suffered a Focus Blast attack
Focus Blast.effect: Use the Move Focus Blast. Use your Attack instead of Special Attack for damage.

Ice Punch.prerequisites: Aura User, has suffered an Ice Punch attack
Ice Punch.frequency: Daily – Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.

Shadow Punch.prerequisites: Aura User, has suffered a Shadow Punch attack
Shadow Punch.frequency: Daily – Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.

Sword of Body and Soul.prerequisites: Aura User, 18 STR, 4 Aura User Features
Sword of Body and Soul.effect: When you take Sword of Body and Soul, choose an elemental Type. You may have Arms attacks you make deal damage of that type.

Thunder Punch.prerequisites: Aura User, has suffered a Thunder Punch attack
Thunder Punch.frequency: Daily – Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.

Battle Chatter.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Battle Chatter.effect: Select and Use one of the following Moves: Taunt, Swagger, or Flatter. Regardless of Move selected, this Feature does not work on the same target twice in an encounter.

Psyche!.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Psyche!.effect: Use the Move Sucker Punch. You may use this Feature as a Free Action when attacked by a Melee-ranged Move or action - if you do, you may not perform a Trainer Action during your next turn.

Ball Buster.target: A Trainer with Pokémon still within Poke Balls
Ball Buster.effect: Make an Arms Feature attack of your choice. If it hits, roll 1d20 and add your ATK modifier. If the result is 16 or higher, one Poke Ball containing a Pokémon that the target possesses is selected at random. That Poke Ball becomes damaged, and cannot be used to release the Pokémon inside for X hours where X is your level. Poke Balls damaged in this way can be repaired instantly by a Capture Specialist or at a Pokémon Center, Poke Mart, Ranger Station or any general place of Law Enforcement.

Barbed Wire.effect: You may only use this feature on the first turn of an encounter. Roll 1d20 and add your ATK mod. If the result is 12 or higher, for the rest of the encounter, attacks you make with the target weapon deal an extra 1d10 damage.

Below the Belt.effect: Use the Move Mega Kick. During the Accuracy Check for Mega Kick, if you roll 14 or higher, the target is Paralyzed.

Concealed Weapon.prerequisites: Dirty Fighter, Weapon of Choice in a small weapon.
Concealed Weapon.effect: Others are not aware you possess your qualifying Weapon of Choice normally. When you first perform an Arms Feature attack using this Weapon of Choice in a combat, your attack has an accuracy check of 2 and ignores the target’s evasion, and doubles its Damage Dice Roll. This bonus does not take place on unarmed attacks.

Crocodile Tears.target: Pokémon or Trainer who dealt damage to you on their last turn.
Crocodile Tears.effect: The next time you roll an Accuracy Check while targeting Crocodile Tears’ target, you need -5 on your Accuracy Check. This effect overrides Think Fast! if both would affect the target. This feature only works once on a target per Encounter.

Desperate Assault.effect: If you have used four different Dirty Fighter Features during a single Encounter, you may use the Move Last Resort.

Face!.frequency: Daily- Every 4 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.

Look There!!!.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Look There!!!.effect: Your target rolls 1d20 and adds either their SATK modifier or their Intelligence Skill as applicable. On a roll of 12 or less, they lose their Trainer Action, for Trainers, or Move, for Pokémon, this round. This Feature does not work on the same target twice in an encounter.

Riposte.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Riposte.trigger: You are attacked with a Melee Arms Feature other than Fists
Riposte.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your ATK modifier. If the result is 16 or higher, the target’s attack fails and their weapon is knocked 5m away. They must spend a trainer action to retrieve a weapon. This may only be used once per turn.

Tax.frequency: Daily- Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Tax.effect: Use the Move Thief. If you target a Trainer with Tax, and you hit with Thief, you may take anything from the Trainer that can be seen on their person.

Think Fast!.trigger: You miss with an Arms Feature or Unarmed attack
Think Fast!.effect: Your action on your next turn that targets the same target requires 3 less on Accuracy Check to succeed.

Yoga Break.effect: After five minutes of work, the target is temporarily at full happiness and appears more appealing. If they level up without a negative incident within an hour of Yoga Break and have met all previous requirements for evolution, evolution is guaranteed.

Pressure Points.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Pressure Points.trigger: You hit with an Unarmed arms attack
Pressure Points.effect: You flinch the target. If the target is successfully flinched, the target becomes immune to further uses of Pressure Points for the rest of the encounter.

Boost Therapy.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Boost Therapy.trigger: You use Yoga Break on a Trainer.
Boost Therapy.effect: Choose a stat. The target of Yoga Break gains +2 to the chosen stat’s modifier for 12 hours. A Trainer may only benefit from one instance of Boost Therapy at a time.

Hand Burst.frequency: Daily – Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Hand Burst.effect: Use the Move Arm Thrust. If the same target is hit 4 times in the same round by Arm Thrust, used with Hand Burst, the target is Paralyzed.

Healing Touch.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Healing Touch.target: Pokémon or Trainers that are not in an encounter.
Healing Touch.effect: After ten minutes of work, the target regains HP equal to 2d12+10 plus your DEF modifier.

Impose Limberness.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Impose Limberness.target: Any adjacent Paralyzed or Flinched Target.
Impose Limberness.effect: The target is cured of their Paralysis and Flinched status.

Invigorating Routine.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Invigorating Routine.effect: The target of Yoga Break gains temporary HP equal to your Defense Stat. In addition, you may now target other Trainers with Massage Therapy.

Nerve Strike.frequency: Daily – Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Nerve Strike.effect: Use an Unarmed Arms Attack. On hit, you paralyze the target.

Precisionist.effect: Whenever you make a Speed check, or make a roll that requires you to add your SPD modifier to your roll for damage, add 5 to your roll.

Relaxation Therapy.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Relaxation Therapy.effect: The target of Yoga Break gains the ability ‘Inner Focus’ for their next encounter that day.

Smelling Salts.frequency: Daily – Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.

Stretch Therapy.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Stretch Therapy.effect: The target of Massage Therapy gains the ability ‘Limber’ for the next encounter that day.

Soothing Song.target: Legendary Pokémon within line of sight.

Tired Tune.effect: Using Tired Tune ends your turn. If this is the third time you used Tired Tune in a row, use the Move Sing before ending your turn. When using Sing, you can choose which targets are unaffected by Sing. The Touched uses musical instruments to play music. Touched characters may not issue commands, unless they have a means to communicate with their Pokémon while playing music (Pokémon may, with Intelligence 4 or greater for example, be able to understand musical cues to use moves, or if you can communicate telepathically, you could also get by this need to play music). The way their Features work depend on your playing music for a set amount of time, then activating the Features actual effects after some set time. You may not use more than one Bard Feature per turn. You may not use the same Bard Feature more than once per turn, either. If you are using a Bard Feature, that says that you may use an effect if you’ve performed the Bard Feature during previous turns, they may be activated multiple turns in a row (Meaning a Bard Feature that activates on its third turn of use, it will also activate on a fourth, fifth or sixth turn afterwards, etc.).

Airy Anthem.effect: Using Airy Anthem ends your turn. If this is the third time you used Airy Anthem in a row, use the Move Whirlwind before ending your turn and you may not use Airy Anthem on your next turn during the next round.

Able Aria.effect: Using Able Aria ends your turn. If this is the third time you used Able Aria in a row, use the Move Aromatherapy before ending your turn.

Bard Feature.effect: Using Heroic Hymn ends your turn. If this is the seventh time you used Heroic Hymn in a row, all adjacent allies gain 2 Combat Stages for any Stat, per ally before ending your turn and you may not use Heroic Hymn on your next three turns during the next three rounds.

Climate Carol.effect: Using Climate Carol ends your turn. If this is the fourth time you used Climate Carol in a row, use the Move Sunny Day, Rain Dance, or Hail before ending your turn.

Daunting Descant.effect: Using Daunting Descant ends your turn. If this is the second time you used Daunting Descant in a row, use the Move Mean Look before ending your turn.

Exhausted Expression.effect: Using Exhausted Expression ends your turn. If this is the second time you used Exhausted Expression in a row, target Pokémon within 10 meters is lowered 1 Combat Stage for the Stat of your choice other than HP before ending your turn.

Nauseous Number.effect: Using Nauseous Number ends your turn. If this is the third time you used Nauseous Number in a row, use the Move Teeter Dance before ending your turn and you may not use Nauseous Number on your next turn during the next round.

Odd Opera.effect: Using Odd Opera ends your turn. If this is the fourth time you used Odd Opera in a row, use the Move Trick Room, Magic Room or Gravity before ending your turn.

Perish Piece.effect: Using Perish Piece ends your turn. If this is the fourth time you used Perish Piece in a row, use the Move Perish Song before ending your turn. Perish Song can only affect Pokémon.

Imposter.frequency: At-Will - Drains 10 of user’s HP per use.
Imposter.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your DEF modifier. If your roll exceeds the target’s Defense or Special Defense, whichever is lowest, you successfully Imitate the target. While Imitating a target, your appearance to others looks exactly like your target. You have a natural insight as to their general demeanor, ways of talking, etc. and can match them near perfectly. Your aura, however, remains as your own. For the next 24 hours, or until you sleep or become unconscious, you have access to that Imitation. You may freely switch between your own form and the form of any imitation to which you have access as a trainer action. For every time you use this feature per day past the first use on any day, lower your HP by 10 if you maintain access to that Imitation. You may, during your turn, give up access to any Imitation as a free action. If you fail to Imitate a target, you cannot attempt that Imitation again for 24 hours. This is an illusion, so those with the ability to see through or dispel illusions will be able to reveal your deception.

Talent Theft.effect: Choose a Feature on the Trainer’s Feature List. This is considered a Borrowed Feature. For the next 24 hours, as long as you maintain access to their Imitation and are physically in their form, you may perform this feature at its normal frequency, or gain its benefits if it is a static feature. You may only Borrow a single feature from any one single source at a time. You may not use Talent Theft to borrow a One-Time-Use feature.

A Little Like You.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
A Little Like You.effect: When you Imitate a Trainer, you may use A Little Like You. Choose a Stat. If the person you are Imitating has a higher Modifier than you in that Stat, your Stat Modifier of the Stat you chose is equal to the Imitated person’s Stat Modifier - 1 as long as you maintain access to their Imitation. This does not change the value of your Stats, only Stat Modifier.

Generic Form.frequency: At-Will - Drains 10 of user’s HP per use.
Generic Form.effect: You are able to change the appearance your entire body at a whim. You change to the gender, haircolor, height, weight, build, and skin tone of your choice. However, you are unable to significantly specify body features and facial structure, with the end result always looking somewhat generic. You are not able to transform to look like anyone in particular, and any attempts to do so will usually be recognizably and unusually off.

I Like Your Bod.prerequisites: Body Snatcher, Just a Little Longer
I Like Your Bod.target: A Trainer whose Imitation you have had access to for at least 72 hours
I Like Your Bod.effect: You create a Body Link with that trainer. You may give up a Body Link during your turn as a free action. As long as you have access to a Body Link, you may attempt to Imitate that target, regardless of distance or line of sight. You may only have one Body Link at a time, plus one more for every 25 trainer levels gained.

It\u2019s Still Mine.effect: Choose a feature you are currently Borrowing. For the next 10 minutes, no one may use that Feature if they are within 10m of you. At the end of this time, you stop Borrowing the feature and lose further access to it.

Just a Little Longer.effect: You no longer lose access to an Imitation due to sleep or unconsciousness. You still lose access to an Imitation after 24 hours are up, unless you lower your Max HP by 10 for as long as you choose to maintain access. If you do, you maintain access to that Imitation for another 24 hours. Maintaining access to an Imitation does not count as performing an Imitation, meaning you may not perform features that trigger upon Imitating a target.

Morphic Control.effect: When you use Impostor to Imitate a target, you may retain your current form if you wish to, instead of taking the target’s form. The Imitation is in all other ways normal, and you still gain access to the Imitation for later use.

Perfect Impostor.prerequisites: Body Snatcher, had successfully used Imposter on 5 people who are not allies
Perfect Impostor.effect: When you Imitate a target, you are able to successfully visually replicate gear and clothing. These are not illusions; these are actual physical replicas However, these replicas do not have any special features that the actual gear or clothing may have, and all useable items do not actually work. For example, you may replicate a potion around their belt, but the potion would have no effect if used. When imitating a target, you are able to successfully mask your aura to appear to be the same as your target’s.

Resonance.effect: Whenever you target an Allied Trainer with Impostor, you automatically pass your check, and you do not lose any HP in order to use Impostor. Additionally, you may access up to three allied Trainer Imitations per day before you must lower your maximum HP.

Talent Mixer.effect: When in your own form or any Imitated form, you may use and apply any Borrowed Features to which you currently have access.

Talent Sponge.prerequisites: Body Snatcher, Talent Theft, used 3 Features that were not your own
Talent Sponge.effect: You may use Talent Theft up to three times per day. You may still only Borrow a single feature from any one single source at a time.

Voice Snatcher.frequency: At-Will - Drains 50 of user’s HP per use.
Voice Snatcher.effect: You make a vocal imprint of the target’s voice. Once you have this imprint, at any time while you are in your own form or the form of any Imitation, you may use the person’s voice. You may collect as many voices as you’d like with Voice Snatcher.

Acupressure.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.

Empower.frequency: At-Will - Drains 10 of user’s HP per use.
Empower.effect: Lower all your Combat Stages to zero. For each Combat Stage you lowered, raise the target’s corresponding Combat Stage by one.

Agility.prerequisites: Conduit, Borrowed Agility or Rock Polish
Agility.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.

Amnesia.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.

Berry Booster.prerequisites: Conduit, has eaten a Liechi, Ganlon, Petaya, Apicot, or Salac Berry.
Berry Booster.frequency: At-Will - Drains 5 of user’s HP per use.
Berry Booster.effect: After five minutes of focus, the targeted Berry gains the ability of a Liechi Berry, Ganlon Berry, Petaya Berry, Apicot Berry, or Salac Berry and loses all other abilities. The flavor and appearance of the Berry do not change, but the Berry counts as a Liechi Berry, Ganlon Berry, Petaya Berry, Apicot Berry, or Salac Berry for the purposes of Berry Eater.

Berry Eater.effect: If you eat a Liechi Berry, Ganlon Berry, Petaya Berry, Apicot Berry, or Salac Berry you are raised 2 Combat Stages instead of 1 when the Berry’s effect is activated.

Conduit Feature.effect: Whenever you have a total of 8 Combat Stages raised, or more, all foes within 10m of you are treated as if a Combat Stage of your choice is lowered 1.

Bulk Up.prerequisites: Conduit, borrowed two Moves that raise Attack or Defense Combat Stages
Bulk Up.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.

Calm Mind.prerequisites: Conduit, Borrowed two Moves that raise Special Attack or Special Defense Combat Stages
Calm Mind.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.

Cosmic Power.prerequisites: Conduit, Borrowed two Moves that raise Defense or Special Defense Combat Stages
Cosmic Power.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.

Iron Defense.prerequisites: Conduit, Borrowed Iron Defense or Acid Armor or Barrier
Iron Defense.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.

Nasty Plot.prerequisites: Conduit, Borrowed Nasty Plot or Tail Glow
Nasty Plot.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.

Swords Dance.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.

Legendary Cry.frequency: Daily - Drains 100 HP of each previously Channeled Legendary Pokémon per use.
Legendary Cry.effect: Use the Move Hyper Voice. For every 100 HP drained to use Hyper Voice, roll an additional 2d10 for damage while using Hyper Voice. You may not use Legendary Cry for 2 days after using Legendary Cry.

Spotcheck.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Spotcheck.effect: For five minutes, you can see from the point of view of any of the Legendary Pokémon you have ever Channeled or possessed. You also know how far each of those Legendaries are from you and which direction they are from wherever you are. Godspeakers must borrow the power of Legendary Pokémon. If they are in good standing with a Legendary, they may request to the Pokémon to make use of their power - the likelihood of the request being granted is up to the GM but should be tied to the Legendary’s current status and the frequency of requests. If the request is not granted, the feature either fails, or the Godspeaker can force it to work. If that is done, the relationship with the Legendary is permanently damaged and may make the Legendary refuse further requests. If this is done too much, the Legendary will begin actively hunting the Godspeaker.

Cold Snap.prerequisites: Godspeaker, Borrowed Ice Beam, Blizzard or Sheer Cold
Cold Snap.frequency: Daily - Drains 100 HP of each previously Channeled Legendary Pokémon per use.
Cold Snap.effect: Use the Move Sheer Cold. For every 200 HP drained in order to use Sheer Cold, subtract 1 from Sheer Cold’s Accuracy Check. You may not use Cold Snap for 5 days after using Cold Snap. After using Cold Snap, you may not use Features, Issue Commands or Shift for 1 minute, or 6 rounds.

Flare.prerequisites: Godspeaker, Borrowed Overheat, Eruption or Fire Blast
Flare.frequency: Daily - Drains 25 HP of each previously Channelled Legendary Pokémon per use.
Flare.effect: Use the Move Overheat. You may not use Flare for 2 days after using Flare.

God Shield.prerequisites: Godspeaker, took 1000 HP from Legendary Pokémon by using Features
God Shield.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.

Kneel.prerequisites: Godspeaker, took 5000 HP from Legendary Pokémon by using Features
Kneel.effect: Use the Move Mean Look. The target may also not Shift for 5 Rounds. The target also may not damage you for 5 Rounds. If the target attacks you or does something that would damage you, you take no damage.

Life Beam.frequency: Daily - Drains 100 HP of each previously Channelled Legendary Pokémon per use.
Life Beam.effect: Use the Move Hyper Beam. You may not use Life Beam for 4 days after using Life Beam. After using Life Beam, you may not use Features, Issue Commands or Shift for 1 minute, or 6 rounds.

Outburst.prerequisites: Godspeaker, Borrowed Outrage, Thrash or Dragon Rush
Outburst.frequency: Daily - Drains 25 HP of each previously Channelled Legendary Pokémon per use.
Outburst.effect: Use the Move Outrage. When you use Outrage with Outburst, do not keep using Outrage on subsequent turns and you do not become confused. You may not use Outburst for 3 days after using Outburst.

Parasitic Embrace.prerequisites: Godspeaker, Borrowed Giga Drain, Mega Drain or Absorb
Parasitic Embrace.frequency: Daily - Drains 25 HP of each previously Channeled Legendary Pokémon per use.
Parasitic Embrace.effect: Use the Move Giga Drain. The HP drained from Legendary Pokémon when this Feature is used counts towards the HP you regain as a result of Giga Drain’s Effect. You may not use Parasitic Embrace for 2 days after using Parasitic Embrace.

Power Mimicry.prerequisites: Godspeaker, took 300 HP from Legendary Pokémon by using Features

Praise Me.prerequisites: Godspeaker, took 500 HP from Legendary Pokémon by using Features
Praise Me.effect: Channelled Pokémon have and may use the Move Helping Hand to target you. Each Pokémon who uses Helping Hand in this way may not benefit from Praise Me for 24 hours.

Tremor.prerequisites: Godspeaker, Borrowed Earthquake, Magnitude or Rock Slide
Tremor.frequency: Daily - Drains 25 HP of each previously Channelled Legendary Pokémon per use.
Tremor.effect: Use the Move Earthquake. Instead of Earthquake having the keyword Burst, it instead has the keyword Blast when used with Tremor. You may not use Tremor for 3 days after using Tremor.

Thunder Call.prerequisites: Godspeaker, Borrowed Zap Cannon, Thunder or Thunderbolt
Thunder Call.frequency: Daily - Drains 25 HP of each previously Channelled Legendary Pokémon per use.
Thunder Call.effect: Use the Move Zap Cannon. You may not use Thunder Call for 3 days after using Thunder Call.

Distraction! Distraction!.effect: If the target uses a Move or Feature that has a Damage Dice Roll on their next turn, they must target you. This may only be used once per turn.

Enough!.prerequisites: Guardian, borrowed Counter or a high Loyalty Pokémon who knows Counter

For the Team.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
For the Team.effect: You may Shift twice as an Interrupt, and Shift between an attacking foe and an allied Trainer or allied Pokémon in order to become the target of a successful Move. You take any damage and effects of the attack as if you were the original target. If you have already Shifted during this round of combat, before using For the Team, you may not Shift during the next round of combat.

Guardian\u2019s Blessing.effect: You may use the Guardian Features Light Screen, Reflect or Safeguard as a Coat that affects only the target, instead of a wall, and lasts until the end of the encounter or the target is recalled.

Not So Fast!.effect: You may use the Guardian Feature Light Screen, Reflect, Safeguard, or Substitute as an Interrupt.

Reflective Resonance.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Reflective Resonance.trigger: You or a Channeled Pokémon are hit by a super-effective attack.
Reflective Resonance.effect: If any Channeled Pokémon resists or is immune to the elemental Type of the Move with a Damage Dice Roll, you or your Pokémon instead take damage as if the attack were neutral.

Repel!.prerequisites: Guardian, borrowed Mirror Coat or a high Loyalty Pokémon who knows Mirror Coat

Safeguard.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature

Savior\u2019s Plight.frequency: Daily - Every 25 levels gained, you may perform this FeaTarget: Self, Trainers, or Pokémon
Savior\u2019s Plight.effect: You may use any Feature that targets only yourself another time per day. ture another time per day. as a free action.

Shared Struggle.target: An adjacent ally trainer or Pokémon is hit by a damaging Move.
Shared Struggle.effect: You take half of the damage that would have been done to your ally. Your ally takes half of the damage it was supposed to take. Pokémon may not apply Defense or Special Defense to damage taken, and trainers, including yourself, may not apply any damage reduction modifiers.

Calling Chord.effect: Roll 1d20. If the roll exceeds 10, an Unown appears within 5 meters of you. If the roll is 20, you may choose the Unown’s letter. It is not immediately hostile. Furthermore, each different Unown symbol counts as a different Pokémon for the purposes of determining Pokémon owned.

Letter Press.effect: You may combine up a total of six separate Unowns into one Pokémon. Combined Unowns retain all different instances of Hidden Power. When using Letter Press on Unown, for each instance of Hidden Power, choose whether it will operate on Attack stat or Special Attack stat. This choice is permanent. When combining Unowns, pick a Unown to be the Prime Unown of that combination. Whenever you add a Unown to a Prime Unown, add 3 points to any one of the Prime Unown’s Base stats. Unowns combined with Letter Press can be kept in a single Poke Ball. Once combined with Letter Press, Unowns cannot be separated.

Banish.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day, you must have 8 Unown with you.
Banish.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your DEF modifier. If the total exceeds 15, the target is removed from the encounter and placed into a pocket dimension known as Unown Space for until the end of your next turn. At that time, the target returns to the exact same spot it was, when it was Banished. The target does not feel any time lost during the it spends in Unown Space as a result of being hit by Banish. You may use Banish only once per encounter on any single target, but you may expend any number of uses of Banish at once to increase the duration of the banishment by 1 round for each use.

Borrow Power.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Borrow Power.effect: Choose an instance of Hidden Power Move on the Channeled Pokémon’s Move List. You may perform this move immediately as a Trainer Action.

Dimension Gate.target: An adjacent area, with nothing occupying the targeted area.
Dimension Gate.effect: Choose an area you have visited in the past year. In the targeted area a magic door opens that, when stepped through, acts as a one-way door, which teleports you to the area you chose. The door teleports you into an open space that is not occupied by anything, or the nearest available area that isn’t occupied by anything. The door remains for up to 5 minutes or until you dismiss it.

Explosive Runes.effect: Expend a use of Borrow Power, and pick an instance of Hidden Power you could have selected with it. You write a sigil of power in Unown script. When a condition, as decided by you, has been met, the sigil explodes in a burst, dealing damage as if you had activated Borrow Power. When a sigil is activated, you become instantly aware of the fact. Only one sigil within 5 meters may detonate at a time; if multiple sigil are triggered, you are still alerted for each one, however. You may have up to X sigil of power active, where X is your DEF modifier.

Extra Eyes.effect: Any time you or a Channeled Pokémon would be targeted by an attack, add one to the target’s Evasion Bonus for each other Channeled Pokémon who is facing the attacker.

Rewrite.effect: After five minutes of focus, choose one of the target’s instances of Hidden Power. You may then choose to reroll the Type for this instance or hidden power, or the base damage for this instance of hidden power.

Ruin.frequency: Daily - As long as you have 14 Active Unowns
Ruin.effect: Make up to three Accuracy Check 5 attack rolls against the same target, each time picking one of Paralyzed, Burned, Poisoned, Asleep, or Confused. If an attack hits, the target is inflicted with the appropriate status.

Sentry Runes.effect: You write a sigil of warding in Unown script. You may, at any time, close your eyes to channel the sigil. While channeling the sigil, you may look and listen through it, and project your voice through it. The sigil may be of any size, but your ability to perceive and communicate through it are proportional to the size of the sigil; tiny writing will allow for a view only as big as a keyhole, for example. If a sigil of warding is destroyed or damaged, you are instantly aware of the fact. You may have up to X sigil of warding active, where X is your DEF modifier.

Unown Arms.frequency: Daily - Every 20 Levels gained, you may perform this feature another time per day.
Unown Arms.effect: The Prime Unown turns into a weapon of your choice. When used with an Arms Feature, an Unown Weapon deals additional damage equal to the Prime Unown’s STAB value. Unown Weapons can be used to block incoming attacks, causing the Prime Unown to take the damage in your stead. This effect lasts up to 24 hours, or until the Prime Unown is knocked out or recalled. Prime Unown cannot use moves or special Skills while in this state.

Wordsmith.effect: You understand all written language. You know the meaning of any word you read, and can fluently read and understand any written passage, even if you cannot speak it.

Possession.frequency: At-Will - Drains 35 of user’s HP per use.
Possession.effect: Roll a Link Check, subtract half of your Defense stat from the result. If you Channel the Pokémon, it is Possessed. While Possessed, you are in complete control of the Pokémon. You may act during an Encounter as any normal Pokémon would. You have access to the Pokémon’s Move List, Abilities and Skills. You may leave a Possessed Pokémon during your turn as a Trainer Action. If you are Possessing a Pokémon and you are captured, you are trapped until the Pokémon is sent out of its Poke Ball, at which point you may remove yourself from the Pokémon. You may not use Trainer Features while Possessing a Pokémon. While Possessing a Pokémon, using a Move counts as your Trainer Action. If the Pokémon you are possessing faints, you may immediately return to your body. If you are Possessing a Pokémon and they are die, you die as well. While you are Possessing a Pokémon, your Body is left unconscious. It cannot make checks and it may not act on its own accord. You may Channel multiple Pokémon with Mystic Features, but you may only Possess one Pokémon at a time.

Possession Borrow.trigger: You leave a Pokémon you were Possessing.
Possession Borrow.effect: Choose a Move on the Pokémon’s Move List, whom you are leaving after Possessing them. This is considered a Borrowed Move. You may perform the Borrowed Move, once, as a Trainer Action during the next 24 hours. A Shaman who is possessing a Pokémon can still make a Trainer Action, can still Issue a Command and may still Shift. To Shift they must use the Possessed Pokémon’s Speed Skills. If during your Trainer Action you use a Feat that enables you to use a Pokémon Move, you use the Possessed Pokémon’s appropriate stats instead of your own. If you use a Feature that requires you to use HP, use the possessed Pokémon’s HP. Your Command works as normal.

Channel Strength.effect: Whenever a Channeled Pokémon uses a Move that damages a target while you are Possessing a Pokémon, add 1d6 to the Damage Dice Roll for each other Channeled Pokémon.

Commune with Nature.effect: Whenever a Pokémon gains experience while you are Possessing them you may sacrifice 10 HP of your Trainer HP. If you do, the Pokémon gains +25% the amount of experience they would gain. You may only apply this Commune with Nature once per encounter.

Dominant Trait.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Dominant Trait.effect: Your mental connection to Pokémon you are Possessing is strengthened; their minds are more difficult to influence. When a Pokémon you are Possessing is afflicted with Infatuation, Confusion or Sleep, you may activate Dominant Trait. Roll 1d20 and add your DEF modifier. If the total exceeds 10 Dominant Trait clears the Status Affliction.

Fangs.trigger: You hit with a Melee Range Move (this can also be while Possessing a Pokémon)
Fangs.effect: Gain HP equal to half the damage dealt with the attack.

Ferocious Embodiment.effect: Whenever a Pokémon you are possessing uses a Move that deals damage to a target, add your DEF modifier to the Damage Dice Roll.

Loan Shark.effect: You may perform Borrowed Moves and Possession Borrowed Moves while Possessing a Pokémon, even if it’s not the Pokémon you Borrowed the Move from.

Natural Selection.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Natural Selection.effect: When you are leaving the body of a Possessed Pokémon, you may use Natural Selection. Choose one of the following Skills. If the Pokémon you are possessing has this Skill, gain that Skill for the next 24 hours: Alluring, Aura, Chilled, Dream Smoke, Firestarter, Fountain, Freezer, Gilled, Glow, Groundshaper, Guster, Heater, Icestep, Inflatable, Magnetic, Materializer, Mind Lock, Pack Mon, Shrinkable, Sprouter, Stealth, Threaded, Tracker, Zapper. Your physical human form changes appropriately per Skill.

Nature Skin.effect: Multiply your DEF modifier by 10 and add it to your total HP. This only applies to your human body. This Feature replaces Mystic Veil.

Primal Surge.effect: The Pokémon you are Possessing has its Speed related Skills increased by X where X is half your DEF modifier.

Share with Nature.prerequisites: Shaman, owned Pokémon who is loyal and has evolved at least once
Share with Nature.effect: When you target a loyal Pokémon you own with Possession, you automatically pass your Link Check.

Shaman Feature.effect: While Possessing a Pokémon, Survival of the Fittest allows you to use any Move on the Possessed Pokémon’s Level Up Move List that could be learned by level X. X is the sum of the Possessed Pokémon’s level and your Level.

Aerokinesis.effect: You may target air up to X meters away with Telekinesis to produce short, but powerful, bursts of air. You can move air rapidly and produce a steady flow of wind that blows at X miles per hour. X is your SATK modifier multiplied by 3.

Electrokinesis.effect: You may target electricity up to X meters away with Telekinesis and redirect it. X is your SATK modifier multiplied by 5. You can also generate electricity in the air with Telekinesis to produce short, but powerful, bursts of electricity. Kinesis Features function without applying your base Telekinesis; they replace Telekinesis when targeting certain elements. In the event that a modifier would be 0 or less when using a Kinesis Feature, your modifier is 1.

Aerodynamics.prerequisites: Air Adept, Defog, a Pokémon with 20 Speed or higher
Aerodynamics.effect: While the weather is Clear, you are treated as if your SPD was one point higher than the trainer immediately before you in action order, but only for the purposes of who acts first, not for evasion or features.

Air Slash.frequency: At-Will – Drains 15 of user’s HP per use.

Charge.frequency: At-Will – Drains 5 of user’s HP per use.

Defog.frequency: At-Will – Drains 10 of user’s HP per use.

Discharge.frequency: At-Will – Drains 10 of user’s HP per use.

Flight.prerequisites: Air Adept, a Pokémon with a Sky Skill
Flight.effect: You are treated as if you have a Sky Speed, using your SATK modifier as the value.

Hurricane.frequency: At-Will – Drains 30 of user’s HP per use.

Magnetic.prerequisites: Air Adept, a Pokémon with Magnetic
Magnetic.effect: You are treated as if you have the Magnetic Skill.

Mass Levitation.frequency: At-Will - Drains 10 of user’s HP per use.
Mass Levitation.effect: While using Flight, you may decrease your maximum Speed Skill by half and lift up to X people. X is equal to your SATK modifier. While using Mass Levitation you can lift any willing target that is lighter than yourself, for every round this Feature is in effect, you must pay 5 additional HP.

Tailwind.prerequisites: Air Adept, 2 features that enable the use of Flying-type moves
Tailwind.frequency: At-Will – Drains 10 of user’s HP per use.

Thunderbolt.frequency: At-Will – Drains 20 of user’s HP per use.

Thunder Wave.prerequisites: Air Adept, 2 features that enable the use of Electric-type moves
Thunder Wave.frequency: At-Will – Drains 20 of user’s HP per use.

Hour\u2019s Past.frequency: At-Will – Drains 5 of user’s HP per use.
Hour\u2019s Past.target: An item, or the exact location you are at.
Hour\u2019s Past.effect: You can view a psychic panel summary of what has happened to that item or area in the past hour. The images are jumbled and might not be chronological order.

Image Tailing.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Image Tailing.target: An image seen while reading an object’s past.
Image Tailing.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SATK modifier. If the total exceeds 15, you can follow a part of the object’s past. You may follow a ghostly image, seen only by yourself, of a person, Pokémon or other object that came into contact with the object you were reading and left. The ghostly image can only be followed for up to a mile from the spot you began to read.

Clairaudience.effect: You can hear sounds that occurred while using Hour’s Past, Day’s Past and Item’s Past. You also can tell when a person is lying to you; unless they believe what they are saying is true.

Clairvoyance.effect: When making an attack, add 3 to any rolls you make. You can see through illusions.

Day\u2019s Past.frequency: Daily – Drains 15 of user’s HP per use.
Day\u2019s Past.target: An item, or the exact location you are at.
Day\u2019s Past.effect: You can view a psychic panel summary of what has happened to that item or area in the past 24 hours. The images are jumbled and might not be chronological order.

Dispel Illusion.effect: You can eliminate an illusion by touching the area it is projected upon. You can see through illusions and disguises but you cannot dispel them for other people and Pokémon to see more than once per day. Although you can see through disguises, you cannot physically force the disguise to be reverted. An example of a disguise would be a Ditto’s transformation. An example of illusions would be Double Team or some other source of magical illusion.

Itemfinder.prerequisites: Clairsentient, an item of your choice to be used for dowsing
Itemfinder.frequency: Daily – Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Itemfinder.target: The exact location you are at.
Itemfinder.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SATK modifier. If the total exceeds 20, you will be guided to a nearby dropped item. The item found will depend on your location.

Item\u2019s Past.frequency: Daily – Drains 24 of user’s HP per use.
Item\u2019s Past.target: An item, or the exact location you are at.
Item\u2019s Past.effect: You can view a psychic panel summary of what has happened to that item or area in the past week. If the item or place has historical significance within the past decade, you are aware of it, but exactly what happened isn’t necessarily clear. This Feature replaces Day’s Past.

Personfinder.target: A person you have met and physically touched before.
Personfinder.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SATK modifier. If the total exceeds 15, you know the direction of a person you are looking for.

Personfinder +.target: A person you have met and physically touched before.
Personfinder +.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SATK modifier. If the total exceeds 15, you know the direction of a person you are looking for and an estimated distance between that person and yourself. This Feature replaces Personfinder.

Psychometry.prerequisites: Clairsentient, an item of your choice to be used for dowsing
Psychometry.effect: You know the purpose of any non-living thing that you touch. You know of any special abilities the item grants. You also know how to activate any item.

Botanokinesis.effect: When targeting any type of plant with Telekinesis, multiply your SATK modifier by 20 pounds instead of 10 pounds. You can rapidly grow any plant you can lift 3 ft per round by subtracting 10 HP from yourself.

Terrakinesis.effect: When targeting any type of rock or piece of ground with Telekinesis, multiply your SATK modifier by 20 pounds instead of 10 pounds. You can shape earth into rock and break up rock into dirt. As long as you are conscious, you and your non-Pokémon allies within 10 meters do not take damage from Sandstorm. Kinesis Features function without applying your base Telekinesis; they replace Telekinesis when targeting certain elements. In the event that a modifier would be 0 or less when using a Kinesis Feature, your modifier is 1.

Aromatherapy.prerequisites: Earth Shaker, two features that allow the use of Grass Type moves
Aromatherapy.frequency: At-Will - Drains 15 of user’s HP per use.

Cotton Spore.frequency: At-Will - Drains 5 of user’s HP per use.

Digger.prerequisites: Earth Shaker, a Pokémon with a Burrow Skill
Digger.effect: You are treated as if you have a Burrow Speed, using your SATK modifier as the value.

Earth Vision.effect: You may now see through rock, dirt, ground, sand etc as if it were not there, for up to X meters, where X is 3 times your SATK modifier.

Giga Drain.frequency: Daily - Drains 15 of user’s HP per use.

Leaf Storm.frequency: At-Will – Drains 25 of user’s HP per use.

Materializer.prerequisites: Earth Shaker, a Pokémon with Materializer
Materializer.effect: You are treated as if you have the Materializer Skill.

Rock Slide.frequency: At-Will - Drains 10 of user’s HP per use.

Sandstorm.frequency: At-Will - Drains 20 of user’s HP per use.

Sand Tomb.frequency: At-Will - Drains 7 of user’s HP per use.

Sand-Attack.frequency: At-Will - Drains 5 of user’s HP per use.

Stone Edge.frequency: At-Will – Drains 25 of user’s HP per use.

Photokinesis.effect: You may target light with Telekinesis and produce more light or take it away. You may remove light from an area completely or spontaneously generate light. Photokinesis has a maximum range of X, where X is your SATK modifier multiplied by 5 meters.

Pyrokinesis.effect: You may target fire up to X meters away with Telekinesis and redirect it. X is your SATK modifier multiplied by 5. You can also generate fire in the air with Telekinesis to produce short, but powerful, bursts of fire. Kinesis Features function without applying your base Telekinesis; they replace Telekinesis when targeting certain elements. If a modifier would be 0 or less when using a Kinesis Feature, your modifier is 1.

Double Team.prerequisites: Fire Breather, a Pokémon who knows the Move Double Team
Double Team.frequency: At-Will – Drains 25 of user’s HP per use.
Double Team.effect: Use the Move Double Team. You may not have more than three copies of yourself at any time. The copies fade after 10 minutes.

Fire Blast.frequency: At-Will – Drains 25 of user’s HP per use.

Fire Spin.frequency: At-Will – Drains 10 of user’s HP per use.

Flash.frequency: At-Will – Drains 5 of user’s HP per use.

Heat Seeker.effect: You can see in the infrared spectrum, allowing you to easily view sources of heat.

Lava Plume.frequency: At-Will – Drains 10 of user’s HP per use.

Mirage.frequency: At-Will - Drains 20 of user’s HP per use
Mirage.effect: You create a visual illusion. You must remain within 10 meters of the Mirage and concentrate to maintain and control the illusion. No one dimension of the illusion may exceed X meters in size, and the illusion persists for X minutes. At the end of this time, you may continue to maintain the illusion by paying Mirage’s activation cost again. X is your SATK modifier. You may only have one Mirage active at any given time.

Night Daze.frequency: At-Will – Drains 20 of user’s HP per use.

Shadow Ball.prerequisites: Fire Breather, 2 Ghost or Dark Type Pokémon
Shadow Ball.frequency: At-Will – Drains 15 of user’s HP per use.

Shimmering Shroud.effect: When a Move that you use via Fire Breather Features modifies accuracy checks, it modifies the check by one more than it would have.

Shadow Walker.prerequisites: Fire Breather, a Pokémon with Invisibility Skill
Shadow Walker.effect: You gain the Invisibility Skill. Activating the Skill costs 15 HP per use. You may not use any Features that do damage or allow you to use Pokémon Moves while Invisible.

Sunny Day.frequency: At-Will – Drains 20 of user’s HP per use.

Cryokinesis.effect: When targeting any type of frozen water with Telekinesis, multiply your SATK modifier by 20 pounds instead of 10 pounds. You can mold ice into other forms of ice and freeze water by thought. As long as you are conscious, you and your non-Pokémon allies within 10 meters do not take damage from Hail.

Hydrokinesis.effect: When targeting any type of water with Telekinesis, multiply your SATK modifier by 20 pounds instead of 10 pounds. You can mold water into other forms of water and produce small quantities of water from the air by thought. Kinesis Features function without applying your base Telekinesis; they replace Telekinesis when targeting certain elements. In the event that a modifier would be 0 or less when using a Kinesis Feature, your modifier is 1.

Blizzard.frequency: At-Will – Drains 25 of user’s HP per use.

Cool Running.effect: You gain the Icestep skill. In addition, you may cross still or slow-moving water by walking on the surface.

Hail.frequency: At-Will – Drains 20 of user’s HP per use.

Haze.frequency: At-Will – Drains 8 of user’s HP per use.
Haze.effect: Use the Move Haze. Rain Waker

Icicle Crash.frequency: At-Will – Drains 10 of user’s HP per use.

Mist.frequency: At-Will – Drains 8 of user’s HP per use.
Mist.effect: Use the Move Mist. Rain Waker

Rain Dance.frequency: At-Will – Drains 20 of user’s HP per use.

Rainy Mood.prerequisites: Rain Waker, Rain Dance or Hail
Rainy Mood.effect: If either you are near a large body of water such as a lake or ocean or the weather is Rain or Hail, you may have water and ice typed Pokémon Moves you use via Rain Waker Features originate at significant sources of water nearby. Significant sources of water are at least enough to submerge a Small sized Pokémon.

Splash Damage.frequency: At-Will - Drains 10 of user’s HP per use.
Splash Damage.trigger: You use an Ice or Water Typed Move
Splash Damage.effect: The Move you use hits an additional adjacent target. If the Move you use already hits an area, instead choose an additional target adjacent to any edge of the area of effect. Activate Splash Damage only once per Move. Splash Damage drains only 5 HP if you are using a Water Typed Move and Rain is active or if you are using an Ice Typed Move and Hail is active.

Surf.frequency: At-Will – Drains 20 of user’s HP per use.

Swimmer.prerequisites: Rain Waker, a Pokémon with Surface and Underwater Speed Skills
Swimmer.effect: You have advanced Surface and Underwater Speeds, adding your SATK modifier to the value of your Overland Speed. You may breathe underwater for X minutes per hour, were X is your Special Attack stat.

Waterfall.frequency: At-Will – Drains 10 of user’s HP per use.

Whirlpool.frequency: At-Will – Drains 7 of user’s HP per use.
Whirlpool.effect: Use the Move Whirlpool. Rain Waker

Faster.frequency: Daily - Every 4 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.
Faster.effect: Your targeted Pokémon’s Speed related Skills are increased by X where X is your SPD modifier until the end of the encounter.

Stronger.frequency: Daily - Every 4 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.
Stronger.effect: For one Round, or one minute out of combat, your targeted Pokémon’s Power Skill is increased by X where X is half your ATK modifier.

Better.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.
Better.target: One of your active Pokémon, under the effect of Smarter.
Better.effect: For one Round, your targeted Pokémon’s Special Attack or Special Defense is increased by one Combat Stage. You may use this Feature multiple times at once, but only to a maximum of 2 Combat Stages in each stat.

Harder.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.
Harder.target: One of your active Pokémon, under the effect of Tougher.
Harder.effect: For one Round, your targeted Pokémon’s Attack or Defense is increased by one Combat Stage. You may use this Feature multiple times at once, but only to a maximum of 2 Combat Stages in each stat.

Higher.prerequisites: Coach, a Pokémon with a Jump Skill of 4 or higher
Higher.frequency: Daily - Every 4 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.
Higher.effect: For one Round, or one minute out of combat, your targeted Pokémon’s Jump Skill is increased by X where X is half your ATK modifier.

Makes Us Stronger.frequency: Daily - Every 15 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.
Makes Us Stronger.target: One of your Pokémon who felled a Pokémon or trainer during the past 3 rounds of combat.
Makes Us Stronger.effect: For one Round, add half of your Attack Stat to your Pokémon’s highest stat. This Feature may grant temporary HP.

Coach Feature.effect: For one Round, the Coach Features: Better, Harder and Quicker, may be used up to 3 times in one round on the same Pokémon instead of being limited to 2 Combat Stages. For one Round, Coach Features that increase Skills double their bonuses.

Never Over.effect: If the Pokémon’s HP is lowered to 0 or less, it may still act and shift but may not use Moves. If they are lowered to -50% HP, they make a Death Saving Throw and this Feature’s effect is cancelled.

Quicker.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.
Quicker.target: One of your active Pokémon under the effect of Faster.
Quicker.effect: For one Round, your targeted Pokémon’s Speed is increased by one Combat Stage. You may use this Feature multiple times at once, but only to a maximum of 2 Combat Stages per stat.

Smarter.prerequisites: Coach, a Pokémon with an Intelligence Skill of 5 or higher
Smarter.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.
Smarter.effect: For one Round, or one minute out of combat, your targeted Pokémon may ignore the penalties for Rough or Icy Terrain, and may move through a space occupied by a hostile Pokémon or Trainers freely.

Tougher.prerequisites: Coach, a Pokémon with a Power Skill of 5 or higher
Tougher.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.
Tougher.effect: For one Round, your targeted Pokémon may ignore any lowered Stats or Combat Stages from Status Effects.

Work It.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day.
Work It.effect: For one Round, all your Pokémon’s Moves with a Damage Dice Roll gain the Push keyword, and Push any targets a number of meters equal to the Pokémon’s Power Skill if the Move uses Attack, or Intelligence Skill if the Move uses Special Attack.

Guardian Sign.frequency: Daily - Every 2 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Guardian Sign.trigger: You take damage from a Move.
Guardian Sign.effect: When taking damage, you are considered the same type as whichever Patron’s Crest you are using.

Healing Sign.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day. Uses 1 Energy Point.
Healing Sign.effect: Roll 2d20 and add your ATK modifier. The target heals that many Hit Points. Whenever you are allied with a Legendary Pokémon, you gain its Crest. Whenever you use a Signer Feature, choose a Crest belonging to one of the Legendary Pokémon you have allied with. Depending on which Pokémon it is, the effects of the other Signer Features may change. When referring to a Legendary Pokémon related to a Crest, Features refer to the Legendary Pokémon as Patrons. When using a Crest, you must have a flat circle, surface, at least 1-meter diameter large to summon the astral form of the allied Pokémon to you. Crests do not inform you of any Patrons’ Moves, or Stats. If any Patrons are reduced to less than 25% of their HP, Signers who are allied with their Patrons will know, if they are thinking about their Patron.

Courage Sign.prerequisites: Signer, a Companion who has been with you for at least 2 weeks
Courage Sign.target: A Helpful Pokémon who is about to leave.
Courage Sign.effect: The target may perform one additional At-Will Frequency Move immediately.

Divine Sign.prerequisites: Signer, a Companion who has been with you for at least 2 weeks
Divine Sign.target: Companion who has been with you for at least 2 weeks
Divine Sign.effect: Choose one of your Crests. The Companion uses a Move that the Patron knows, which is not a Signature Move, as chosen by your GM (The GM should choose an appropriate Move for the situation). This counts as Issuing a Command. This may only be used once per turn.

God Sign.target: Companion who has been with you for at least 1 month
God Sign.effect: Choose one of your Crests. The Companion uses a Signature Move, chosen by your GM, which the Patron knows. A Signature Move being a Move which is known almost exclusively by that Patron. This counts as Issuing a Command.

Grand Sign.frequency: Daily - Every 6 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day. Uses 1 Energy
Grand Sign.effect: Add your ATK modifier and your SPD modifier together. Add that total to the Helpful Pokémon’s next damage-dealing Move. This may only be used once per turn.

Restoring Sign.effect: Roll 3d20 and add three times your ATK modifier. The target heals that many Hit Points.

Revealing Sign.frequency: Daily - Every 3 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Revealing Sign.effect: The Restrained “Helpful” Pokémon has four Moves revealed to you by your GM that the Restrained Pokémon knows. If the Pokémon knows less than four Moves, each Move is revealed.

Righteous Sign.effect: All allies with line of sight to you may pick one Stat, other than HP, and raise its Combat Stage twice.

Smite Sign.effect: While attacking with a Trainer Attack, you may activate a Crest. When activated, choose one of the Patron’s Elemental Types and treat your attack as that Elemental Type.

Signer Feature.effect: Choose a Crest, you may not use your Styler for the remainder of the encounter. Next to you a miniature, astral form of your Patron appears. It is medium sized. It has HP equal to your ATK stat multiplied by 5. It is identical to the Legendary in every other way including Skills, Abilities and Moves. Your GM will determine its stats. It is considered Helpful until it performs 10 tasks that would usually dismiss another Helpful Pokémon. If the astral Patron is attacked it does not counts as something that would otherwise dismiss a Helpful Pokémon for the count of 10 tasks before the astral Patron disappears.

Swift Sign.prerequisites: Signer, a Companion who has been with you for at least 2 weeks
Swift Sign.effect: You may immediately issue a command to the target, even if you have commanded another Pokémon during that round of an encounter. The Helpful Pokémon is dismissed as usual afterwards.

Specialists Styler.effect: You upgrade your Styler to your own personal specifications, granting it 30 maximum energy. Spec Operations Rangers commonly use special gear distributed by Ranger Stations. Ranger Stations expect gear to be returned in good shape and may expect a fee if the gear is somehow damaged. The prerequisites specify any specific gear that may be needed to be borrowed. More than four items of Special Operations gear are not usually lent out to a single Ranger at the same time. Special Operations Members may be called by local enforcement if help is needed for a major incident.

Coiled Styler Upgrade.prerequisites: Spec. Ops. Member, Cable with Two-Way Motor
Coiled Styler Upgrade.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this feature another time per day. Uses 3 Energy Points.
Coiled Styler Upgrade.effect: Use a ranged instance of the Move Wrap up to a range of 4 meters. You may also use the 40 meters of cable located somewhere on your person is fed through the Styler upgrade, with the options of a grappling hook, magnetic weight, or adhesive solution on the cable’s end. The motor can pull your weight and up to an additional 200 pounds. Each use of the move Wrap uses 4 points of energy from your Styler.

Crowd Control Upgrade.prerequisites: Spec. Ops. Member, Crowd Control Adapter
Crowd Control Upgrade.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day. Uses 3 Energy Points.
Crowd Control Upgrade.effect: Use the move Sleep Powder or Smokescreen with a maximum range of 5 meters. Using this feature spends 4 points of energy from your Styler.

Crowd Suppression Upgrade.prerequisites: Spec. Ops. Member, Crowd Control Adapter
Crowd Suppression Upgrade.frequency: Daily - Every 6 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day. Uses 3 energy Points.
Crowd Suppression Upgrade.effect: Use the move Spider Web or Stun Spore with a maximum range of 4 meters. Using this feature spends 5 point of energy from your Styler.

Elemental Stone Upgrade.prerequisites: Spec. Ops. Member, Elemental Stone Catalyst Adapter
Elemental Stone Upgrade.effect: You insert an evolutionary stone into the adapter to draw out various powers. Once inserted, these stones lose the potency to evolve a Pokémon, but can continued to be used in this equipment. You may only use one stone at a time, and it takes one minute to change which stone is attached. Using this feature spends 5 points of energy every day unless used with a Thunder Stone. Gain the Skills for appropriate stone which is used: Stone Skill Dawn Stone Glow Dusk Stone Mind Lock Fire Stone Firestarter (No manipulation) Leaf Stone Juicer Moon Stone Stealth Shiny Stone Glow Sun Stone Alluring Thunder Stone Adds an additional 10 points to Styler maximum energy Water Stone Fountain

Lock-On Upgrade.effect: Use the Move Lock-On. You receive the targets maximum HP and current HP through your scanner. Using this feature spends 2 points of energy from your Styler.

Protective Armor.prerequisites: Spec. Ops. Member, Light Personal Armor
Protective Armor.effect: When taking damage from attacks, take 5 less damage. This Armor can be hidden under most jackets and multilayer clothing.

Perfect Armor.prerequisites: Spec. Ops. Member, Light Personal Armor, Protective Armor
Perfect Armor.effect: When taking damage from attacks, take 15 less damage. You may only factor in one other source of damage reduction along with Perfect Armor. While using Perfect Armor, you do not benefit from Protective Armor.

Protect.prerequisites: Spec. Ops. Member, Endure, Protective Armor
Protect.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day. Uses 5 Energy Points.
Protect.trigger: You are hit by a Move.

Rebreather.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Rebreather.effect: Use the water rebreather, allowing you to breathe underwater for a maximum of 45 minutes per use. After the rebreather is used to its full time, it must be cleaned and dried, rendering it unable to be used for two hours.

Take Down.prerequisites: Spec. Ops. Member, Light Personal Armor
Take Down.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.

Portable Dream Machine.effect: You have a Dream Machine, comparable in size to a laptop. When used on a sleeping Pokémon, this machine will allow you to see their dreams through a viewing window for up to 8 hours, after which, the Pokémon will wake up. During this time, the Pokémon is “in the Dream World.” If the Pokémon is disturbed during its dream, it must roll a normal sleep check as if it was Asleep. Dreams will show any subconscious desires or wishes the Pokémon has, although the dream’s true meaning may be obscure. A Pokémon may only be observed by the Dream Machine once per three days.

Dream Smoke Materializer.frequency: At-Will - As long as an owned Pokémon with Dream Smoke is working with you
Dream Smoke Materializer.target: A Pokémon who has been asleep in the Dream World for 4 hours.
Dream Smoke Materializer.effect: Roll 1d20 plus your SDEF modifier. On a roll of 18 or above, the Pokémon will find an item in their dream world and hold onto it, and then immediately wake up. The Pokémon will awake holding the item they dreamed about. Pokémon can only dream about a specific item if they have held it before. The dreamt item is chosen by the GM.

Dream Training Montage.target: A Pokémon who has been asleep in the Dream World for 7 hours.
Dream Training Montage.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. On a result of 20 or better, you may take X amount of points from one of the Pokémon’s base stats and apply it to a different base stat. X is equal to your half of you SDEF modifier. If this changes the Base Relation, you must place points where appropriate while levelling to right the Base Relation as soon as possible. Dream Training Montage may not target a Pokémon who has been successfully targeted by Dream Training Montage before.

Lucid Dreamers.effect: Add 3 to any check while using a Feature on a Pokémon in the Dream World.

Naptime.prerequisites: Dream Doctor, one Pokémon has remained in the dream world for 8 full hours.
Naptime.target: Dreaming Pokémon in the Dream Machine.
Naptime.effect: The amount of time required for features requiring a specific amount of time asleep is reduced by 1/4th hours, rounding up.

Naptime +.target: Dreaming Pokémon In the Dream machine.
Naptime +.effect: The amount of time required for features requiring a specific amount of time asleep is reduced by 1/3rd, rounding up. This feature replaces Naptime.

Pokewalker.prerequisites: Dream Doctor, True Ability, Dream Training Montage
Pokewalker.target: A Pokémon in the Dream World.
Pokewalker.effect: While a Pokémon is in the Dream World, you may place your Pokewalker, an item about the same size as a pedometer, into a port on the Dream Machine. You may place the Pokémon’s subconscious dream into the Pokewalker and immediately return a Pokémon to its Poke Ball. While a Pokémon’s subconscious dream is in the Pokewalker, it gains 500 experience points per 24 hours it remains in the Pokewalker with you and its Poke Ball. These experience points are given to the Pokémon when they are sent out of their Poke Ball, next to your Dream Machine with the Pokewalker in its Dream Machine port. You may target the subconscious of the Pokémon in the Pokewalker with Dream Doctor Features within the eight hours of your putting a Pokémon into the Pokewalker. If a Pokémon whose subconscious is in the Pokewalker is sent out of its Poke Ball without the Pokewalker and Dream Machine ready, the subconscious dream disappears from the Pokewalker and no experience points are rewarded to the Pokémon. You cannot use your Dream Machine for anything else while a Pokémon is in a Pokewalker.

Psychotherapy.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Psychotherapy.target: Dreaming Pokémon in the Dream Machine.
Psychotherapy.effect: Roll 1d20 plus SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 15, you can understand why a Pokémon is upset, what the source of the unhappiness is, and what needs to be done in the real world to fix it. You may ask questions to the sleeping Pokémon and the Dream Machine will accurately reveal answers to the best of the Pokémon’s ability.

Subtle Suggestion.prerequisites: Dream Doctor, successfully used Dream Smoke Materializer 5 times
Subtle Suggestion.target: Dreaming Pokémon while using Dream Smoke Materializer.
Subtle Suggestion.effect: Declare the name of an item the Dreaming Pokémon has seen and used before, roll 1d20 and add SDEF modifier. On a result of 20 or better, the Pokémon will dream about that item and it will materialize if the Dream Smoke Materializer is successful.

Temporary Ability.target: A Pokémon who has been in the Dream World in the past week.
Temporary Ability.effect: Randomly choose an Ability the target has; the target loses that Ability for 1 hour. Randomly assign an Ability from the target’s Ability list, the target gains that Ability for 1 hour. The Randomly assigned Ability can potentially grant the same Ability that the Pokémon just lost for 1 hour, changing nothing.

Total Recall.effect: Ignore the lines “A Pokémon may not have more than two Abilities after using True Ability. If the targeted Pokémon already has two Abilities when using True Ability, on a successful roll, replace one of the Abilities,” while using True Ability. This means that a Pokémon can have 3 Abilities after using True ability successfully.

True Ability.target: A Pokémon who has been asleep in the Dream World for 8 hours.
True Ability.effect: Roll 1d20 plus your SDEF modifier. On a result of 17 or better, assign an additional Ability to the Pokémon from the Pokémon’s Basic Abilities. On a result of 21 or better, assign any additional Ability to the Pokémon from the Pokémon’s High Abilities. A Pokémon may not have more than two Abilities after using True Ability. If the targeted Pokémon already has two Abilities when using True Ability, on a successful roll, replace one of the Abilities. True Ability may not target a Pokémon who has been successfully targeted by True Ability before.

Walking Doctorate.target: Anything related to knowledge you can look up.
Walking Doctorate.effect: You know Pokémon data, relating to Pokémon in your Pokédex, about specific location and base stats. You may consult the Pokédex whenever you want about anything. Restricted to nonLegendary Pokémon.

Flawless Classification.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.
Flawless Classification.effect: Roll d20 and add your SATK and SDEF modifiers to the roll. If you roll higher than 15, you know which stat is the highest for the targeted Pokémon and which stat is the lowest. If you roll higher than 20, you know the exact values for those stats.

Critical Advice.frequency: Daily – Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day.
Critical Advice.effect: During the next round of an encounter, all Moves and Trainer Attacks are Critical Hits on 1620. The Critical Hit range may not be altered in any other way during the next round of combat.

Demoralize.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day.
Demoralize.effect: Pick either Attack, Defense, Special Attack, Special Defense or Speed and lower the chosen stat of each enemy Pokémon’s within X meters of by 1 Combat Stage. X is your SATK modifier.

Efficiency Boost.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day.
Efficiency Boost.effect: During the next round of an encounter, any action that an ally performs that requires a d20 roll may add X to their roll where X is your SATK modifier. Cannot be used with Moves that set targets to a specific amount of HP.

Fighting Read.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day.
Fighting Read.effect: Roll d20 and add your SATK and SDEF modifiers to the roll. If you roll higher then 15, your GM must give to you 6 Pokémon species of which 4 are owned by the target trainer. If the target does not own 4 Pokémon, only two Pokémon which are owned are revealed.

Morale Boost.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day.
Morale Boost.effect: Pick either Attack, Defense, Special Attack, Special Defense or Speed and raise the chosen stat of each of your ally’s Pokémon’s within X meters of you by 1 Combat Stage until the end of the encounter. X is your SATK modifier

Morale Rally.effect: Pick either Attack, Defense, Special Attack, Special Defense or Speed and raise the chosen stat of each of your ally’s Pokémon’s within X meters of you by X. X is your SATK modifier. Morale Rally lasts until the end of the encounter.

Poke Linguist.effect: You can speak and understand a single species of Pokémon and all the evolutions related to that Pokémon. You may take Poke Linguist more than once.

Sabotage.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SATK modifier. If you rolled above 15, shift all wild foes up to X meters where X is your SATK modifier or your SDEF modifier.

Tactical Maneuver.frequency: Daily – Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day.
Tactical Maneuver.target: One of your Pokémon who just successfully used a Move.
Tactical Maneuver.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SATK modifier. If you rolled above 15, an allied Trainer’s Pokémon may perform an At-Will Move or EOT Move even if it has already used a Move during that round of the encounter. The target may shift an additional time. You must have line of sight to the Pokémon who is receiving your extra command.

Tactical Strike.frequency: Daily – Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day.
Tactical Strike.target: One of your Pokémon who just successfully used a Move.
Tactical Strike.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SATK modifier. If you rolled above 15, an allied Trainer’s Pokémon may perform an At-Will Move even if it has already used a Move during that round of the encounter. You must have line of sight to the Pokémon who is receiving your extra command.

Attack Order.description: Attack Order is a Critical Hit on 18-20 during Accuracy Check.

Bug Bite.description: If the target has a consumable Held Item, Bug Bite destroys the Item and Bug Bite’s user may use the Item’s effect.

Bug Buzz.description: Bug Buzz creates a 3-meter Burst. Bug Buzz lowers the target’s Special Defense 1 Combat Stage on 19-20 during Accuracy Check.

Defend Order.description: Raise the user’s Defense 1 Combat Stage and raise the user’s Special Defense 1 Combat Stage.

Fell Stinger.description: If the user lowers a target to 0 HP or less while using Fell Stinger, raise the user’s Attack 2 Combat Stages.

First Impression.description: If a foe wants to declare a Move, you may instead use First Impression on their turn before they can use their Move. First Impression may not be Interrupted. First Impression may only be used if no other Moves have been performed during this encounter.

Fury Cutter.description: If Fury Cutter is used successfully and consecutively on the same target, use 1d12+6 on the second hit instead, 2d12+12 on the third hit instead, 3d12+18 on the fourth hit instead and 4d12+24 on the fifth hit and any successful hits following the fifth hit instead.

Heal Order.description: The user regains HP equal to half of its full HP.

Infestation.description: The target gains the Coat, Infested. Infested Pokpmon lose 1/8th of their full HP at the beginning of each of their turns for 5 rounds. Do not apply weakness, resistance or stats to the lost HP. Infested Pokpmon are Trapped and cannot move more than 3 meters from the user.

Leech Life.description: After the target takes damage, the user gains HP equal to half of the damage they dealt to the target.

Lunge.description: Lower the user’s Attack 1 Combat Stage after damage.

Megahorn.description: The target is Pushed 3-meters. The target takes an additional 1d6 if it is Pushed into Blocking Terrain. If the target is pushed into another Legal Target, instead, both take 1d6.

Pin Missile.description: Pin Missile can hit up to 5 times. Once the user misses, they cannot attempt to make another Pin Missile attack on that turn.

Pollen Puff.description: If the target is the user’s ally, instead of dealing damage, Pollen Puff heals the target after calculating damage.

Powder.description: The target receives a Powder Coat. If the target uses a Fire-Type Move, the Coat is destroyed and they lose 1/4th of their max HP.

Quiver Dance.description: Raise the user’s Special Attack 1 Combat Stage, raise the user’s Special Defense 1 Combat Stage and raise the user’s Speed 1 Combat Stage.

Rage Powder.description: Rage Powder creates a Column 1 meter wide. All legal targets may only target the user when using a Move until the user is knocked out or switched out. This Effect lasts for one round of combat.

Signal Beam.description: Signal Beam creates a Column 1 meter wide. Signal Beam confuses the target and any Legal Targets on 19-20 during Accuracy Check.

Silver Wind.description: Silver Wind creates a Column that is 2meters wide. If you rolled 11-20 during Accuracy Check for Silver Wind, the user has each of its stats raised 1 Combat Stage. If you rolled 16-20 on the Accuracy Roll, all allies in the Column also have each of their stats raised 1 Combat Stage and are not damaged by Silver Wind.

Steamroller.description: Steamroller Flinches the target on 15-20 during Accuracy Check. If the target is Small, Steamroller deals an additional 1d10.

Sticky Web.description: Set 10 square meters of Sticky Web, all 10 meters must be adjacent with at least one other space of Sticky Web next to each other. Sticky Web cause all foes to have their Speed Combat Stage lowered 1 when moving onto or over the occupied spaces with an Overland Skill. A foe can only lose 1 Combat Stage per turn this way.

String Shot.description: String Shot creates a Column 2-meters wide. All Legal Targets within the Column lower their Speed 1 Combat Stage. If a target is hit by String Shot 5 times within 5 rounds of combat, they are Trapped. If a target’s Speed Combat Stage has already been lowered 6 times, String Shot Traps them.

Struggle Bug.description: Struggle Bug creates a Column 1 meter wide. Struggle Bug lowers all Legal Targets Special Attack 1 Combat Stage. Dark Moves

Tail Glow.description: Raise the user’s Special Attack 3 Combat Stages.

Twineedle.description: Twineedle can hit up to 2 times. If Twineedle misses its first target, you may still use it a second time. If you rolled an 18-20 on either Accuracy Check, the target is Poisoned.

U-Turn.description: If U-Turn successfully hits its target, the user deals damage and then immediately is returned to its Poke Ball in the same turn. A New Pokpmon may immediately be sent out.

Assurance.description: If Assurance’s target has already been damaged by a Move on the same round Assurance is being used, use 4d12+16 instead for Assurance’s Damage Dice Roll.

Beat Up.description: 1d8+X - Battle - 2 Beat Up can hit up to X times. X is the amount of Pokpmon on the user’s roster including the user, up to a maximum of 6. Once the user misses, they cannot attempt to make another Beat Up attack on that turn. For each target, use a different Pokpmon’s Attack stat from your roster to calculate damage.

Bite.description: Bite Flinches the target on 15-20 during Accuracy Check.

Crunch.description: Crunch lowers the target’s Defense 1 Combat Stage on 17-20 during Accuracy Check.

Dark Pulse.description: Dark Pulse creates a 3-meter Burst. Dark Pulse Flinches all Legal Targets on 17-20 during Accuracy Check.

Dark Void.description: Dark Void creates a 10-meter Burst. All Legal Targets fall Asleep.
